title: Create counterfactual failure-analysis skill over ee lab evidence
description:
## Background

Counterfactual interpretation is useful but belongs in an agent skill. The Rust CLI should provide episode capture, frozen replay, pack diffs, and hypotheses; the skill should help an agent reason about what might have changed and what evidence would falsify the claim.

This preserves the value of the counterfactual lab without making `ee lab` pretend to know how an agent would have behaved.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should require `ee lab capture/replay/counterfactual --json` outputs as evidence. It should gate conclusions on replay quality, redaction status, missing inputs, pack diffs, and provenance. It should label outputs as hypotheses unless backed by replay evidence and explicit validation.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/counterfactual-failure-analysis/` or an equally clear project-local name.
- `SKILL.md` defines triggers, required `ee` commands, stop/go gates, output contract, and failure modes.
- Skill distinguishes observed facts, replay evidence, hypotheses, assumptions, and agent judgment.
- Skill refuses to conclude `would have succeeded` from pack presence alone.
- Includes references/templates for failure analysis memo, evidence checklist, and falsification checklist.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, required `ee lab` evidence commands, stop/go gates, template fields, refusal behavior for weak evidence, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill script runs against deterministic lab fixtures covering no evidence, replay-supported failure, contradictory replay, redacted evidence, prompt-injection-like evidence, and degraded `ee lab` output.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, all `ee` commands, degraded states, evidence IDs, evidence bundle path/hash, redaction status, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Implemented in commit f56ac4d: counterfactual failure-analysis skill, references, fixtures, and local validation script. JSONL sync is pending because .beads/issues.jsonl is currently reserved by another pane.

title: Gate 20: Memory Economics Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE uses economy scores to demote artifacts, compact context packs, or recommend prune plans.

Create memory economy contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/memory_economy.rs
tests/golden/economy/report.json
tests/golden/economy/score_memory.json
tests/golden/economy/budget_release.json
tests/golden/economy/simulate_budget.json
tests/golden/economy/prune_plan_dry_run.json
tests/golden/economy/tail_risk_reserve.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee economy report --json` reports utility, attention cost, maintenance debt, false-alarm cost, and tail-risk reserve status
- `ee economy score <id> --json` explains one artifact's score with evidence and uncertainty
- `ee economy prune-plan --dry-run --json` never mutates memories, procedures, tripwires, situations, files, or indexes
- high-severity safety artifacts remain available through tail-risk reserve even when ordinary usage evidence is sparse
- sparse evidence produces abstain or review recommendations rather than aggressive demotion
- simulated budgets are deterministic on fixed fixtures and do not change current ranking state
- economy outputs validate against exported schemas and preserve stdout/stderr isolation

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design: Implement or verify the exact tests, fixtures, schemas, and acceptance criteria named in the gate text. If the gate cannot pass because an upstream dependency is not ready, record an explicit br comment with the blocker and degraded/deferred posture.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

The gate-specific contract/golden/evaluation tests pass; stdout/stderr and schema behavior are verified where relevant; any claimed feature has evidence linked in comments or the close reason.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.
notes: Gate bead generated from the readiness-gate section of COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_TO_MAKE_EE.md. It is intentionally self-contained and includes the relevant gate text.
close_reason: Gate 20 contracts verified: cargo test --test contracts economy_gate20 passed (3 tests). Dependency audit clear; fmt/clippy all-targets currently blocked by unrelated in-flight workspace changes (src/db/mod.rs formatting + clippy too_many_arguments in tests/usr003_in_task_scenario.rs).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Post-close evidence: test(contracts): harden Gate 20 assertions in tests/contracts/economy_gate20.rs (commit ec48cf4). Re-verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test contracts economy_gate20 (3 passed).

title: EE-177: Add Mermaid diagram golden tests for graph, why, doctor, and curation outputs
description:
Source plan item: EE-177 from the `Scientific Analytics And Diagrams` backlog.

Task: Add Mermaid diagram golden tests for graph, why, doctor, and curation outputs

Background and intent:
Add optional offline science analytics and deterministic diagram exports for evaluation and diagnostics without slowing the default agent loop.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-017, EE-169. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Tests are part of the deliverable, not a follow-up. Golden output should be deterministic with fixed IDs, timestamps, ordering, and schema versions.
- Graph data is derived and explainable. Stale or unavailable graph features must degrade honestly without breaking lexical/manual memory workflows.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
- If a dependency or upstream Franken project is not ready, implement the smallest honest diagnostic/degraded path rather than faking behavior.
- Document any architectural exception with an ADR-oriented note and a verification hook.

title: Create rehearsal and promotion-review skill over dry-run artifacts
description:
## Background

Reviewing a rehearsal, deciding whether to promote changes, and planning rollback/verification steps is agent judgment. The CLI should produce real dry-run artifacts or honest degraded reports; a skill should interpret those artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should consume `ee rehearse plan/run/inspect/promote-plan --json` only after the CLI side is honest. It should review artifacts, identify missing preconditions, and produce a conservative promotion checklist.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/rehearsal-promotion-review/` or equivalent.
- Workflow requires real rehearsal artifacts or degrades/refuses when `ee rehearse` is unavailable.
- Output includes artifact summary, mutation risks, preconditions, verification steps, rollback considerations, and go/no-go recommendation for the agent/human.
- Skill never authorizes destructive commands; it may recommend asking the user for explicit approval where needed.
- Includes templates for rehearsal review and promotion checklist.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, rehearsal-artifact gates, promotion checklist fields, unavailable/unsupported refusal behavior, destructive-command escalation wording, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill fixture covers successful dry run, partial failure, unavailable rehearsal, unsupported command, redacted artifact evidence, malformed rehearsal artifact, and degraded CLI output cases with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, rehearsal artifact IDs/paths, evidence bundle path/hash, mutation posture, redaction/degraded status, go/no-go class, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: this skill owns rehearsal/promotion-review interpretation over real dry-run artifacts or explicit unavailable reports from nd65. It must not treat planned artifacts as executed evidence and needs skill e2e cases for missing, contradictory, redacted, and degraded rehearsal inputs.

title: EE-HANDOFF-001: Add session handoff and resume capsule command family
description:
Define and implement an agent-native session handoff and resume capsule command family.

Background:
EE is meant to serve coding agents across real work, not just answer isolated memory queries. In practice, agents lose context because sessions end, model context windows fill, a task changes hands, or another agent needs to continue from the same workspace. The current bead graph has strong foundations for memory, CASS import, context packs, recorder runs, outcome feedback, support bundles, and a multi-workspace/session-continuity scenario, but it lacks a concrete reusable handoff artifact for routine continuation.

This bead adds a normal-operation continuity capsule. It is different from the diagnostic support bundle: a support bundle explains what went wrong; a handoff capsule tells the next agent what is happening, what evidence matters, what to do next, what not to repeat, and which memories/context items justify that plan.

Intended public surface:
- `ee handoff create --json --out <path> [--since <run-or-time>] [--profile resume|handoff|compact]` writes an explicit redacted continuity capsule and prints a machine-readable manifest.
- `ee handoff preview --json [--since <run-or-time>] [--profile resume|handoff|compact]` reports the capsule plan, selected evidence, token/byte estimates, redaction posture, and excluded sources without writing the capsule.
- `ee handoff inspect <path> --json` validates an existing capsule, schema version, hashes, redaction status, workspace scope, and included evidence.
- `ee handoff resume <path|latest> --json` renders the next-agent resume payload from a capsule without mutating EE state.

The capsule is not a backup, not a raw transcript export, not a support bundle, and not an automatic task executor. It is a compact, provenance-rich state transfer optimized for agents.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive unit tests, e2e tests, schema/golden fixtures, privacy checks, and detailed logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- Define a strict `handoff_capsule.v1` schema with deterministic sections: workspace identity, repository fingerprints, agent/session/run IDs, current objective, status summary, active blockers, recent decisions, accepted outcomes, pending next actions, do-not-repeat guidance, selected memories/context items, evidence IDs, command/run summaries, artifact pointers, redaction summary, and degradation posture.
- Build capsule content from existing services rather than creating a parallel memory system: workspace detection, recorder run/event spine, CASS-derived sessions, context packs, search/context selection reasons, outcome feedback, procedure/tripwire/preflight signals when available, and support-bundle pointers only when diagnostics are explicitly requested.
- Require every included claim to have provenance: memory ID, session/evidence span, recorder event ID, context pack record, outcome ID, closure dossier, or explicit generated summary source. Summaries without evidence should be marked as low-confidence and excluded from action recommendations unless a profile explicitly allows draft hints.
- Keep profiles explicit and deterministic. `compact` optimizes for a small next prompt, `resume` optimizes for immediate continuation, and `handoff` optimizes for complete transfer to a different agent. All profiles should report omitted sections and why.
- `resume` must be read-only by default. It should not claim tasks, edit files, import sessions, apply memories, run shell commands, or update outcome state. If future mutation is useful, it should be a separate explicit command with dry-run/audit behavior.
- Use the same stdout/stderr discipline as the rest of EE: stdout is only the canonical JSON response; progress, skipped evidence, redaction events, ambiguity diagnostics, and first-failure diagnosis go to stderr or referenced artifacts.
- Make ambiguity visible. If multiple workspaces, repositories, sessions, or recent runs match, return a stable ambiguity code with candidate IDs and next commands instead of guessing.
- Preserve privacy and scope boundaries. A capsule for one workspace must not silently include another workspace's memories or session evidence unless the scope is explicitly requested and represented in the manifest.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- `ee handoff preview --json` returns an `ee.response.v1` payload with planned capsule sections, source evidence IDs, scope filters, profile, redaction posture, token/byte estimates, degraded/unavailable sources, omitted sections, and whether the capsule would be sufficient for a next-agent resume.
- `ee handoff create --json --out <path>` writes a deterministic redacted capsule only under the explicit output path and prints only the manifest response to stdout.
- `ee handoff inspect <path> --json` validates schema version, content hashes, workspace identity, repository fingerprints, section completeness, evidence references, redaction summaries, profile compatibility, and supported version ranges.
- `ee handoff resume <path|latest> --json` emits a compact next-agent payload with current objective, immediate next actions, blockers, do-not-repeat guidance, selected context/memory IDs with reasons, recent decisions/outcomes, and artifact pointers without mutating EE state.
- The capsule distinguishes evidence-backed facts, inferred summaries, stale/low-confidence hints, and explicit unknowns. It must not present inferred or stale content as verified fact.
- Scope is explicit for every included memory, context item, session, recorder event, and artifact. Multi-workspace ambiguity returns a stable degradation/ambiguity code and candidate resolution commands rather than silently choosing.
- The capsule never includes raw DB files, raw unredacted transcripts, raw secrets, unrelated workspace files, or user-global config secrets by default.
- Dry-run/preview, inspect, and resume are read-only. Create mutates only the explicit output path and records that artifact write in command-effect expectations.
- Capsules remain useful under degraded conditions: missing search index, unavailable CASS import, absent recorder run, stale context pack, or unavailable optional analytics must produce honest partial sections with stable codes and next actions.
- Public schemas and golden fixtures cover preview, create, inspect, resume, compact profile, handoff profile, multi-workspace ambiguity, partial degraded sources, redaction failure, stale evidence, missing artifact, and hash mismatch.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add unit tests for capsule section selection, evidence provenance validation, deterministic ordering, profile-specific omissions, token/byte estimation, manifest hashing, redaction summary aggregation, scope filtering, ambiguity detection, stale evidence classification, and read-only command-effect classification.
- Add contract/golden tests for every public response shape and the `handoff_capsule.v1` schema, including valid and invalid fixtures with readable diffs.
- Add real-binary e2e coverage that creates two isolated workspaces with overlapping vocabulary, records/imports session evidence, builds context, creates a capsule, inspects it, resumes from it, and proves the wrong workspace's evidence is not selected silently.
- Add an interruption/resume e2e scenario where an in-progress task is captured after decisions and failed commands, then resumed in a fresh environment with the same next actions, blockers, do-not-repeat guidance, and evidence links visible.
- Add degraded/offline e2e fixtures for missing CASS, stale search index, absent recorder events, and unavailable optional analytics; the capsule must remain useful and include stable codes with next actions.
- Add privacy regression tests with synthetic secrets in session-like text, command output, config-like values, and memory bodies; raw synthetic secrets must not appear in stdout, capsule manifests, resume payloads, inspect output, or failure summaries.
- Add adversarial/property coverage for path traversal in `--out`, malformed capsule files, duplicate evidence IDs, invalid UTF-8/control characters, huge transcripts, stale hashes, symlinked output paths, and concurrent capsule creation attempts.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, capsule path, manifest hash, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, command-effect classification, selected/rejected workspace IDs, selected/rejected evidence IDs, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; capsule planning details, skipped evidence explanations, redaction events, ambiguity diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit capsule-local artifacts.
- Closeout evidence must include fixture IDs, scenario IDs, manifest hashes, command-effect manifest entries, exact verification commands, and the reason this command remains distinct from support bundles and backups.
notes: Added during repeated bead quality review after searches showed scenario-level continuity coverage but no concrete routine handoff/resume capsule command or schema. This bead improves agent ergonomics for context exhaustion, session interruption, multi-agent handoff, and future self-resume without conflating normal continuation with diagnostic support bundles or raw exports.

title: Create session review and memory-candidate distillation skill
description:
## Background

Session review and memory-candidate distillation are valuable, but they require agent judgment: deciding what a session taught us, which facts are durable, what is a rule versus an anecdote, and what evidence is strong enough. That work should be a project-local skill that consumes CASS/`ee` evidence and emits candidate artifacts for the mechanical curation CLI.

This preserves the README workflow around `ee review session --propose` and `ee curate candidates` without embedding hidden AI reasoning in the Rust binary.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design:
Create a skill that runs around mechanical evidence commands such as `ee import cass --dry-run --json`, `ee memory show --json`, `ee search --json`, `ee context --json`, and curation candidate validation outputs. The skill should produce review memos and candidate JSON/YAML files that can be fed to `ee curate validate/apply`; it should not directly alter the DB.

The skill should include strict sections for observed facts, candidate memories, rejected/non-durable observations, assumptions, evidence pointers, confidence rationale, and follow-up validation commands.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/session-review-memory-distillation/` or an equally clear project-local name.
- `SKILL.md` defines triggers, required `ee`/CASS evidence commands, stop/go gates, output templates, uncertainty handling, privacy/redaction handling, and degraded behavior.
- Output separates observed session facts, candidate memories/rules, anti-patterns, rejected observations, assumptions, evidence URIs, confidence rationale, and follow-up `ee curate validate/apply` commands.
- Skill refuses to produce high-confidence procedural rules without provenance and validation plan.
- Includes templates for session review memo, candidate memory file, rejection log, and validation checklist.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, evidence-command references, template fields, refusal behavior for missing provenance, degraded CASS/`ee` handling, prompt-injection-like transcript handling, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill script covers empty session, noisy session, prompt-injection-like transcript, duplicate candidate, strong procedural candidate, redacted evidence, and degraded CASS/`ee` outputs with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, CASS/`ee` command transcript, evidence bundle path/hash, redaction/trust/degraded fields, output artifact path, required-section check, recommended follow-up commands, and first-failure diagnosis.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: this skill carries session-review judgment outside the Rust binary. It must consume explicit ee/CASS evidence and produce candidate artifacts for mechanical curation, not mutate the DB directly or promote high-confidence procedural rules without provenance and validation gates.

comments.text: While verifying EE-040, offloaded 'cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings' reaches EE-023 test code and fails on src/config/workspace.rs:295 with clippy::single_match in discover_handles_root_path_without_panicking. I am not editing the EE-023 work surface without coordination; this blocks a clean all-targets clippy report for EE-040.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented by SilentMountain. Adds src/config/workspace.rs with deterministic upward .ee/ lookup.

API:
- WORKSPACE_MARKER constant ('.ee')
- struct WorkspaceLocation { root, config_dir } + WorkspaceLocation::new(root) helper
- enum WorkspaceError { CurrentDir(io::Error) } with std::error::Error::source chain
- discover(start: &Path) -> Option<WorkspaceLocation> (purely lexical, no panics)
- discover_from_current_dir() -> Result<Option<WorkspaceLocation>, WorkspaceError>

Discipline:
- closest-marker-wins (cargo/git convention)
- no canonicalisation; relative paths stay relative
- Path::is_dir is the only I/O, follows symlinks naturally
- termination guard: parent == current escape for empty-path case
- no new deps (test harness uses uuid which is already in tree)

10 unit tests with a tiny built-in scratch-dir helper cover: direct match, nested walk-up, closest-wins for nested workspaces, no-marker -> None, .ee-as-file ignored, root path / terminates, empty path '' terminates, relative-path non-canonicalisation, WorkspaceLocation::new consistency, discover_from_current_dir success-or-structured-error.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --check on src/config/workspace.rs: clean
- Local cargo test 118/118 pass before unrelated cli/mod.rs breakage from another agent's clap-based work

Note for EE-005 author: src/cli/mod.rs currently has 4 clap-related compile errors in tests (binary == on Result<(...,clap::error::Error), _>). My module's correctness is unaffected; flagging here so the cli author can clean up their test comparisons.

comments.author: SwiftBasin
comments.text: Applied the mechanical clippy cleanup in src/config/workspace.rs for discover_handles_root_path_without_panicking (single_match -> if let) because it was blocking all-targets clippy while EE-040 verification was running and Agent Mail was unavailable. No behavior change intended.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented EE-414: added dry-run-only `ee procedure promote <procedure-id> --dry-run --json` through the normal procedure service/output/CLI surfaces. The command now builds a deterministic promotion report with pending curation candidate details, planned audit event details, planned durable effects marked unapplied, verification summary, warnings, and next actions. Non-dry-run promotion is explicitly policy-denied with a stable repair command instead of silently mutating memory.

Coverage added: core unit tests for dry-run report and non-dry-run denial, CLI JSON envelope/error tests, and a real-binary smoke test asserting the curation/audit plan contract.

Verification: `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` passed; `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test procedure_promote --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` completed remotely with exit 0 on ovh-b (local artifact retrieval later hung, so I terminated only the completed rch wrapper after recording the remote success); `git diff --check -- src/cli/mod.rs src/core/procedure.rs src/models/procedure.rs src/output/mod.rs tests/smoke.rs` passed.

UBS note: `ubs src/cli/mod.rs src/core/procedure.rs src/models/procedure.rs src/output/mod.rs tests/smoke.rs` still exits 1 due a locationless hardcoded-secret heuristic. Manual diff scan for secret/password/token/key/bearer/auth patterns found no actual secret-like material, and UBS otherwise reported fmt/clippy/check/test-no-run clean. The earlier count-cast warning was fixed with checked/saturating conversion.

Agent Mail note: mailbox list/inbox remains unavailable due the existing malformed mailbox DB/resource-busy failures, so completion coordination is recorded here in Beads.

title: EE-415: Add procedure drift detection from failed verification, stale evidence, and dependency contract changes
description:
Source plan item: EE-415 from the `Procedure Distillation And Skill Capsules` backlog.

Task: Add procedure drift detection from failed verification, stale evidence, and dependency contract changes

Background and intent:
Turn repeated successful traces into verified reusable procedures and render-only skill capsules while preserving evidence and avoiding silent installation.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-383, EE-412. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Keep scope narrow but complete: implement the named behavior, wire it into the relevant command/schema/test surfaces, and avoid unrelated refactors.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
- If a dependency or upstream Franken project is not ready, implement the smallest honest diagnostic/degraded path rather than faking behavior.
- Document any architectural exception with an ADR-oriented note and a verification hook.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The behavior described by `Add procedure drift detection from failed verification, stale evidence, and dependency contract changes` is implemented or, for spikes, conclusively reported with a clear go/no-go recommendation.
- The implementation preserves local-first, no-Tokio, no-rusqlite, agent-native CLI behavior.
- Public command output, if any, validates against the stable response envelope and does not pollute stdout with diagnostics.
- Relevant unit, integration, golden, contract, or evaluation tests are added and named so future agents can find them.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and the relevant test subset pass after implementation.
- Any degraded or unavailable dependency state returns a stable error/degradation code with a useful next action.
- No silent durable mutation is introduced; write paths have explicit audit, dry-run, idempotency, or curation behavior as appropriate.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.

title: Gate 18: Procedure Distillation Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE advertises reusable procedures, skill capsules, or generated playbooks as more than draft artifacts.

Create procedure contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/procedure_distillation.rs
tests/golden/procedure/propose_release.json
tests/golden/procedure/show_candidate.json
tests/golden/procedure/verify_pass.json
tests/golden/procedure/verify_fail_drift.json
tests/golden/procedure/export_markdown.md
tests/golden/procedure/export_skill_capsule.json
tests/golden/procedure/promote_dry_run.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee procedure propose --json` generates candidates only when evidence support clears configured thresholds
- generated procedures include preconditions, ordered steps, stop conditions, verification commands, and evidence IDs
- `ee procedure verify --json` can pass and fail deterministically against fixtures or repro artifacts
- failed verification downgrades the procedure to `needs_revalidation` without deleting prior evidence
- export commands are render-only and never install hooks, edit project files, or promote memories unless a separate explicit command is used
- skill-capsule export preserves the same procedure ID, evidence IDs, warning labels, and verification state as JSON
- promotion dry-run routes through normal curation, audit, idempotency, and trust checks
- procedure outputs validate against exported schemas and preserve stdout/stderr isolation

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design: Implement or verify the exact tests, fixtures, schemas, and acceptance criteria named in the gate text. If the gate cannot pass because an upstream dependency is not ready, record an explicit br comment with the blocker and degraded/deferred posture.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

The gate-specific contract/golden/evaluation tests pass; stdout/stderr and schema behavior are verified where relevant; any claimed feature has evidence linked in comments or the close reason.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.
notes: Gate bead generated from the readiness-gate section of COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_TO_MAKE_EE.md. It is intentionally self-contained and includes the relevant gate text.

title: Create claim and certificate review skill over mechanical manifest verification
description:
## Background

Hash/schema verification is mechanical, but deciding whether a claim is persuasive, stale, overbroad, or suitable for documentation requires judgment. This skill preserves the useful review workflow without putting that reasoning into `ee claim` or `ee certificate`.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should consume mechanical `ee claim` and `ee certificate` reports, then produce a review memo that separates verified artifact facts from analyst judgment and required follow-up evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/claim-certificate-review/` or equivalent.
- Workflow requires real manifest/certificate outputs before qualitative review.
- Output includes verified facts, failed/stale checks, assumptions, overclaim risks, missing evidence, and follow-up commands.
- Skill refuses to strengthen a claim when hash/schema verification is missing or degraded.
- Includes templates for claim review and certificate evidence checklist.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, evidence-gate behavior, manifest/certificate template fields, stale/failed/refusal wording, follow-up command rendering, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill fixture covers verified, stale payload, stale schema, expired certificate, missing manifest, failed assumption, redacted evidence, malformed verification output, and degraded verification cases with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, claim/certificate IDs, manifest paths, evidence bundle path/hash, hash/schema/expiry status, redaction/degraded status, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: this skill owns claim/certificate review over mechanical manifest verification from v76q. Rust verifies files, hashes, schema/status/expiry/assumptions, and degradation; the skill may interpret claim posture only from those artifacts and must refuse unsupported certainty.

title: EE-USR-004: Implement post-task outcome, curation, procedure, and learning scenario
description:
Implement the user-outcome scenario for turning a completed task into useful future memory without unsafe automatic promotion.

Required flow:
- Record outcome feedback for memories, rules, preflights, context packs, or procedures.
- Propose curation candidates from a session and validate/apply only through explicit review boundaries.
- Distill a procedure candidate where evidence supports it and verify pass/fail behavior on fixtures.
- Feed closed outcomes into learning/economy/counterfactual signals without rewriting raw evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- This scenario should prove EE can learn conservatively: evidence first, proposal second, explicit apply last.
- Include a harmful or false-alarm example so demotion/negative evidence is as well-supported as promotion.
- Keep procedure export render-only unless a separate explicit mutating command is under test.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Claimed on main. Agent Mail inbox/list-agents still fail with malformed SQLite, and send_message to MistySalmon returned temporarily busy, so this comment is the coordination record. I reserved src/core/index.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, tests/golden.rs, and tests/smoke.rs. src/db/mod.rs is reserved by MistySalmon, so I will avoid DB edits and implement re-embedding job support using the existing search_index_jobs table contract.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Implemented re-embedding job support on main without editing src/db/mod.rs, which was reserved by MistySalmon. Added ee index reembed with JSON/TOON/human renderers, dry-run without queuing, deterministic idempotency_key, stable embedding summary, and durable search_index_jobs lifecycle using the existing FullRebuild job shape with document_source NULL and embedding_scope=all_documents. Rebuilds Frankensearch via the existing hash embedder fallback and records completed/failed/no-documents status. Added focused core tests plus a real-binary smoke test that runs init, remember, index reembed --dry-run --json, index reembed --json, and verifies the persisted job. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; rch cargo check --all-targets passed; rch cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; rch cargo test index_reembed -- --test-threads=1 passed; forbidden dependency audit reported ok; git diff --check passed; TMPDIR=/var/tmp ubs src/core/index.rs src/cli/mod.rs tests/smoke.rs exited 0 with no criticals (heuristic warnings remain in broad pre-existing surfaces). Agent Mail list/fetch still fails with database errors, so this Beads comment is the coordination record.

With TMPDIR=/data/tmp, rch exec cargo test memory_poisoning --test eval_fixtures passed 3/3 and returned locally. No code changes made for this spike.

title: EE-CONTEXT-PERSIST-001: Wire `ee context` command path to persist pack records
description:
Follow-up/regression bead for the walking skeleton: `ee context` currently assembles a `ContextResponse` but the command path does not insert a durable `pack_records` row or item/omission rows. EE-151 closed the database/repository layer and explicitly noted that command wiring was blocked. That means `ee why` and pack-hash inspection can only explain packs seeded by tests or other paths, not normal user-visible context packs.

This bead wires the real command path so every successful non-dry-run `ee context` invocation persists its pack record, selected items, omissions, profile, query, token counts, degradation metadata, deterministic pack hash, and creator/audit context.
design: Use the existing pack record repository APIs rather than inventing a second persistence path. Build the persisted input from the same `ContextRequest` and assembled draft returned to the caller, so stdout and storage cannot diverge. The implementation should remain deterministic and should degrade honestly if storage cannot persist after search/pack succeeds.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- `ee context --json` over an initialized workspace with persisted memories inserts exactly one `pack_records` row for the command invocation.
- `pack_items` preserve selected memory IDs, ranks, token estimates, scores, reasons, provenance, and section/profile metadata in stable order.
- `pack_omissions` preserve deterministic omission reasons when the pack budget excludes candidates.
- The persisted pack hash matches the hash returned/rendered by the context response.
- `ee why <memory-id> --json` can report the latest normal-command pack selection without test-only seeding.
- Storage failure after successful assembly returns a stable degraded/error code and never corrupts partial pack rows.
- Real-binary e2e covers `init -> remember -> index rebuild -> context -> why` and fails if pack records are absent.
- Unit/integration tests cover empty results, degraded search, duplicate invocation boundaries, deterministic ordering, and rollback/partial-write behavior.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and relevant golden/e2e tests pass before closure.
close_reason: Pack record persistence wired into run_context_pack. Uses blake3 hash, persists items and omissions, degrades gracefully.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for context pack persistence. Keep the existing scope, and make these details explicit during implementation:

- This command is intentionally read-plus-durable-pack-write, not read-only. The command-effect manifest must declare the pack record, item, omission, audit/effect, and derived metadata writes.
- Persist pack records in one transaction or equivalent all-or-nothing repository operation; failures must leave no orphaned pack_items/omissions and must return stable storage/degraded codes.
- Repeated identical context invocations may create distinct pack records, but the pack hash and selected item ordering must remain deterministic for the same DB/index/config/query/profile.
- Store enough request metadata for later `ee why` and support bundles: workspace, query text/query doc hash when present, profile, max token budget, format, degraded capabilities, selected IDs, omitted IDs, and explanation factors.
- Unit tests must cover pack-hash determinism, transaction rollback, selected/omitted item ordering, empty pack behavior, degraded search/index state, and why lookup over persisted command-created packs.
- Real-binary e2e logs must include before/after pack row counts, pack_id, pack_hash, selected memory IDs, why lookup ID, stdout/stderr paths, schema/golden status, effect expectation/observed delta, redaction status, and first-failure diagnosis.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space polish: removed the parent-child epic edge to avoid the open Context Packing epic being treated as an implementation blocker by bv/br ready-work selection. Epic association is preserved with the epic-context-packing/context-packing labels; real technical dependencies remain explicit as blocks edges.

title: EE-QUERY-SCHEMA-001: Add `docs/query-schema.md` and pin `ee.query.v1`
description: README.md advertises `ee pack --query-file task.eeq.json --max-tokens N --format toon` and links to `docs/query-schema.md`, but the docs file is missing and the CLI has no query-file contract. This bead defines the versioned `ee.query.v1` request document before implementation proceeds, using the useful EQL ideas from the legacy Eidetic projects while preserving this repo's constraints.
design: Make the schema a contract, not an aspirational prose page. It should cover text query, tags and negative tags, boolean metadata filters, time/as-of filters, graph/neighborhood hints, labels for eval, explain flags, output profile/format hints, pagination/cursor posture where relevant, and deterministic validation errors. Keep JSON canonical; TOON/Markdown are renderers. Do not introduce custom retrieval math; execution must map to DB filters, Frankensearch, and optional graph projections.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- `docs/query-schema.md` exists and documents `ee.query.v1` fields, defaults, validation errors, examples, and non-goals.
- Public schema export includes `ee.query.v1` or a clearly named query-document schema with stable field names.
- Examples include simple text, tag filtering, metadata boolean filters, time/as-of use, graph hints, eval labels, and explain output.
- The docs specify deterministic ordering, cursor/pagination behavior, unknown-field policy, and redaction/security posture.
- The docs explicitly state that execution must use existing DB/search/graph modules and must not implement custom RRF/BM25/vector fusion.
- Golden/schema tests fail if the exported schema drifts unexpectedly.
- A follow-up implementation TODO is listed for CLI `--query-file` plumbing if it is not completed in this bead.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for `ee.query.v1`. Keep the schema expressive rather than shrinking it to a single text query:

- Include `version`, `workspace`, `query.text`, positive/negative tags, structured metadata filters, time/as-of filters, temporal-validity posture, graph/neighborhood hints, trust/redaction filters, output/profile hints, explain/debug flags, eval labels, limit/budget fields, cursor posture, and extension/unknown-field policy.
- Document which fields are selection filters versus ranking/explanation hints so users can predict behavior. Do not let graph or temporal hints silently hide memories unless the query explicitly requests that behavior.
- Define stable error codes for malformed JSON, unknown version, unknown field, invalid timestamp, invalid operator, unsafe path/reference, incompatible fields, and unsupported-but-recognized future features.
- Add schema/golden tests plus parser/property/fuzz tests using adversarial agent-facing inputs from the existing fuzz harness.
- Add examples that compose temporal validity, link traversal, trust filters, and explain output; examples must be executable or covered by golden fixtures once query-file plumbing lands.
- The docs must explicitly name the implementation substrate: DB filters + Frankensearch + optional graph projection, never custom retrieval math.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space review note after concurrent close: this bead was closed while polish was still being applied. Before treating it as fully done, verify the implementation and closeout evidence against comment 214 and the labels `unit-tests-required`, `logged-e2e-required`, and `schema-golden-required`. If schema/golden/parser/property coverage or executable examples are missing, reopen this bead or file a narrow follow-up before implementing query-file plumbing.

title: EE-QUERY-FILE-001: Implement query-file pack/context CLI plumbing
description: Implement the command plumbing promised by README after `ee.query.v1` is pinned. The current CLI has `ee context`, but README advertises `ee pack --query-file task.eeq.json --max-tokens N --format toon`; there is no `Pack` command or query-file path. This bead reconciles the public command surface with the schema contract.
design: Prefer one canonical service path for context/pack generation. If `ee pack` is added, it should be an alias or thin command layer over the same core pack service as `ee context`, not a divergent implementation. Query documents must validate before storage/search work begins and must produce stable JSON errors. Keep `--query-file` read-only with respect to the input file.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- CLI supports the documented query-file workflow either as `ee pack --query-file ...` or an ADR-backed corrected command surface that updates README consistently.
- Query documents validate against `ee.query.v1` with stable JSON error codes for missing query, invalid filters, unsupported future versions, malformed JSON, and unsafe paths.
- Successful query-file invocation delegates to the existing context/pack assembly service and preserves pack persistence behavior.
- `--format toon`, `--json`, `--fields`, and stdout/stderr isolation behave consistently with other machine-facing commands.
- Real-binary golden tests cover valid query, invalid JSON, unknown field policy, unsupported version, and no terminal styling in machine output.
close_reason: Completed query-file pack CLI plumbing and verification evidence recorded in bead comment 259.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for query-file CLI plumbing:

- Prefer matching the README command surface exactly (`ee pack --query-file ...`) unless implementation discovers a hard architectural reason; if so, update README/docs and add an ADR note in the bead closeout explaining why the public contract changed.
- Treat `--query-file` as a read of an explicit user-provided path only. Validate path existence, file type, size limit, UTF-8/JSON parse, schema version, unknown fields, and unsafe symlink/path traversal posture before any search, pack, or storage work.
- The successful path must flow through the same context/pack service and pack-record persistence as normal `ee context`; no forked pack logic and no different ranking semantics.
- Unit tests must cover schema loading, validation error mapping, command-effect classification, option precedence (`--json`, `--format`, `--fields`, max tokens), and no mutation on validation failure.
- Real-binary golden/e2e tests must cover valid query file, invalid JSON, unsupported version, unknown field policy, unsafe path, missing file, large file rejection, format variants, persisted pack row, and stdout/stderr isolation with logged artifacts.

Implemented query-file pack CLI plumbing is present and validated: ee pack --query-file delegates through the existing context/pack path, validates ee.query.v1, reports stable JSON errors for malformed JSON, unsupported versions, missing files, invalid filters, unsafe paths, unsupported future fields, and zero budgets, and preserves stdout/stderr isolation. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Reopened after cross-agent plan review. The previous close reason explicitly left durable storage unwired because it depended on EE-044; EE-044 is now closed, so `ee remember` continuing to emit `storage_not_implemented` and "Memory was not persisted" is a walking-skeleton regression.

Granular TODO for implementation:
- Route `ee remember` through the real workspace resolution and FrankenSQLite/SQLModel memory repository.
- Preserve existing validation for level, kind, confidence, tags, source/provenance URI, and dry-run.
- In non-dry-run mode, insert a durable memory row, tags, provenance/audit records, and any revision/idempotency metadata required by the current repository contracts.
- In dry-run mode, prove no DB rows, index jobs, audit rows, or derived artifacts are created.
- Remove the `storage_not_implemented` degradation from successful persisted output; only report real degraded states.
- Keep JSON stdout parseable and stable; diagnostics and repair hints must stay out of machine stdout except in structured degradation/error fields.
- Add/repair real-binary e2e coverage for `ee init; ee remember --json; ee memory show; ee search/index/context` over an isolated workspace.
- Include regression coverage that fails if `remember` claims success while not persisting.
- Run `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and relevant real-binary/golden tests before closure.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: NobleCardinal claimed EE-062. Scope: wire ee remember to durable FrankenSQLite storage using existing audited memory repository, preserving validation/dry-run and adding focused tests. Current git status is clean on main at 8075ef89, matching origin/main.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for EE-062. Preserve the existing implementation TODO and add these closure requirements:

- Treat persisted `ee remember` as the first walking-skeleton gate: after `ee init`, a non-dry-run remember must create durable memory, tags, provenance, audit/effect metadata, and either enqueue/refresh the derived search document or report an explicit stale-index degradation with a next action.
- Validate policy before durable storage: redaction/trust/source/provenance checks must run before content can become source of truth, and synthetic secret fixtures must prove raw secrets are not persisted or emitted.
- The JSON response should include the durable memory ID, revision/version, workspace ID, audit/effect IDs where available, index/degradation status, and stable `suggestedLinks` placeholder behavior for future adjacency suggestions.
- Unit tests must cover repository insert/read, tag normalization, provenance validation, dry-run/no-write, idempotency or duplicate-warning behavior, audit/effect recording, stale-index reporting, and rollback on partial failure.
- Real-binary e2e logging must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized env, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr paths, DB row counts before/after, memory ID/revision, audit/effect IDs, schema/golden status, redaction status, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Closure should include the exact verification commands and artifact paths so a future agent can reproduce the walking-skeleton proof.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space review note after concurrent status churn: `EE-062` briefly showed a close reason of `done` while this polishing pass was adding stricter criteria, and is now back in progress. Final closeout should include concrete evidence for comments 208 and 212: durable memory rows, policy/redaction proof, audit/effect metadata, stale-index behavior, real-binary e2e artifacts, and exact verification commands. A bare close reason is not sufficient for this P0 walking-skeleton bead.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: NobleCardinal progress update: implemented durable ee remember path through workspace .ee/ee.db with validation, dry-run/no-write behavior, memory/tag/provenance insert, audit entry, queued search index job, stable JSON metadata fields (revision_number, index_status, effect_ids placeholder, suggested_links placeholder, redaction_status), and pre-storage secret-like content policy denial. Added unit coverage for dry-run no DB, persisted memory/audit/index job, and secret-like policy rejection; updated smoke e2e for remember -> memory show -> index rebuild -> search -> context and improved first-failure output. Verification so far: cargo fmt --check passes; earlier remote RCH cargo check and clippy passed before concurrent conflict churn; remote RCH cargo test --lib remember_memory passed 3 tests. Current blockers: Agent Mail fetch/list/send still fail from shared mail SQLite corruption; RCH intermittently fail-opens cargo commands locally despite remote-ready status, and local fallback attempts were stopped; concurrent work introduced unmerged/conflict state in cass/config/db/graph/search, which I resolved to marker-free working content and staged to clear unmerged status. Latest remote smoke/check reruns are blocked or failing on remaining concurrent merge artifacts/RCH instability, so I am not closing EE-062 yet.

title: Final graph polish and implementation-readiness review for mechanical-boundary epic
description:
## Background

The user asked to work in plan space first and check the beads carefully before implementation. This final bead is the explicit polish pass: review the new graph, revise weak beads, verify dependencies, and ensure the plan is optimal for users without oversimplifying or losing functionality.
design: Use `br` and `bv` only for bead graph changes/diagnostics. Re-read the relevant project instructions, command inventory, and this epic. Update descriptions, acceptance criteria, dependencies, or comments where the graph is unclear or suboptimal.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance criteria

- Every child bead under the mechanical-boundary epic is reviewed for self-containment, scope, dependencies, test coverage, and user value before implementation starts.
- Missing command surfaces, user workflows, fixture needs, mutation/idempotency contracts, runtime/cancellation requirements, or baseline infrastructure surfaces discovered during review are added as beads rather than silently folded into vague acceptance criteria.
- Weak or duplicate beads are revised with `br update`; dependency direction is corrected when it would block the wrong phase of work.
- A plan-space unit/static coverage check confirms every open or in-progress mechanical-boundary implementation/skill bead explicitly names unit/static tests, e2e/docs/e2e scripts, and detailed logs; any plan-only exception is listed in a comment.
- `br dep cycles` returns no cycles after revisions.
- `bv --robot-insights`, `bv --robot-triage`, `bv --robot-plan`, and relevant `bv --robot-suggest` output are run and summarized.
- Final comment on the epic summarizes the ready implementation order, known risks, intentionally deferred scope, accepted/rejected `bv` suggestions, and any existing beads that still need reconciliation.
- No implementation starts until this bead closes or the user explicitly overrides.

comments.text: Final graph polish summary from FoggyCrane. Diagnostics run: br dep cycles --json returned count 0; bv --robot-insights computed PageRank, betweenness, HITS, eigenvector, critical path, cycles, k-core, articulation, and slack with no cycles; bv --robot-triage reports 82 open, 40 actionable, 42 blocked, 6 in_progress; bv --robot-plan identifies a mechanical-boundary Track A of eidetic_engine_cli-71ep, eidetic_engine_cli-frv3, eidetic_engine_cli-jp06, eidetic_engine_cli-rp3m, and eidetic_engine_cli-s7vd plus broader roadmap Track B; bv --robot-suggest returned noisy old-roadmap duplicate/label/dependency hints; bv --robot-priority recommended lowering several P0 mechanical beads, which I reject because the priority reflects the user's explicit reality-check concern and the mechanical-boundary plan, not just immediate direct-unblock count. Ready implementation order: first finish the cross-cutting mechanical contracts that feed all command migrations: eidetic_engine_cli-frv3 command-surface coverage matrix, eidetic_engine_cli-rp3m e2e logging standard, and eidetic_engine_cli-jp06 degraded-honesty coverage; then run eidetic_engine_cli-s43e side-effect/idempotency contracts, eidetic_engine_cli-uiy3 deterministic fixture/golden corpus, and eidetic_engine_cli-v6h4 cancellation/budget tests; in parallel, eidetic_engine_cli-71ep dependency/AI-surface audit and eidetic_engine_cli-s7vd project-local skill standards can proceed; after that, command-family rewiring beads such as eidetic_engine_cli-6xzc, qmu0, bijm, q5vf, dz00, db4z, ynzg, tzmg, 5g6d, 6956, 6cks, ve0w, v76q, nd65, and evah become safer to implement. Known risks: jp06 now has reusable fake-success machinery and one E2E slice but still needs broad command-family coverage; project-wide fmt/clippy are currently blocked by unrelated unowned test files; old roadmap duplicate/missing-dependency suggestions should be handled by eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2 rather than folded into this mechanical-boundary gate. Accepted suggestions: no cycle fixes needed; use Track A as the immediate parallelization plan; treat nj16 as a separate high-impact non-mechanical ready gate. Rejected suggestions: do not down-prioritize the mechanical-boundary P0 beads merely because their direct-unblock count is low; do not apply mass duplicate/dependency suggestions generated from generic roadmap wording without human review. Closing this plan-space bead so implementation can continue under the explicit user override and this recorded order.

title: Gate 16: Prospective Preflight And Tripwire Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE claims it can warn agents before likely repeated mistakes.

Create preflight and tripwire contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/preflight_tripwires.rs
tests/golden/preflight/release_task.json
tests/golden/preflight/degraded_evidence.json
tests/golden/preflight/false_alarm_close.json
tests/golden/tripwire/list.json
tests/golden/tripwire/check_match.json
tests/golden/tripwire/check_no_match.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee preflight "<task>" --json` emits a compact risk brief with top risks, ask-now prompts, must-verify checks, tripwires, evidence IDs, and next action
- preflight can run before a context pack exists
- high-severity tripwires survive ordinary field projection and token budgets
- `ee tripwire check --json` is read-only and deterministic over a fixed event payload
- stale or missing evidence is reported with `preflight_evidence_stale` or `tripwire_inputs_incomplete`
- closing a preflight records helped, missed, stale, and false-alarm outcomes without deleting evidence
- repeated false alarms can lower future priority while preserving high-severity safety rules
- preflight output never claims that a warning will prevent failure; it only reports risk, evidence, and recommended checks
- all preflight and tripwire outputs validate against exported schemas and preserve stdout/stderr isolation

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

title: Gate 12: Toon Rust Output Adapter Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before `--format toon`, `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon`, or TOON examples in agent docs are considered stable.

Create TOON output contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/toon_output.rs
tests/golden/toon/health.toon
tests/golden/toon/status.toon
tests/golden/toon/search_minimal.toon
tests/golden/toon/context_standard.toon
tests/golden/toon/why.toon
tests/golden/toon/doctor_fix_plan.toon
tests/golden/toon/roundtrip_context.json
tests/golden/toon/malformed_input_error.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee-output` compiles with the local `/dp/toon_rust` package as `toon = { package = "tru", path = "../toon_rust", default-features = false }`
- default `ee-cli` feature tree still excludes Tokio, Hyper, Axum, Tower, Reqwest, async-std, smol, `rusqlite`, SQLx, Diesel, SeaORM, and `petgraph`
- `--format toon` emits only stdout data and never writes progress, warnings, or rich rendering to stdout
- each TOON golden fixture decodes through `toon::try_decode` with strict mode and matches the canonical JSON envelope for the same command
- `--json` remains JSON even when `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon` is set
- `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon` affects only ordinary agent-native commands with no explicit format and never affects hook or MCP protocol output
- malformed TOON in diagnostic fixtures returns `toon_decode_failed`, and renderer failures return `toon_encoding_failed`
- `ee capabilities --json` reports whether TOON output is available, the resolved `toon` dependency source, and the supported output profiles
- output-size diagnostics report JSON bytes, TOON bytes, estimated JSON tokens, estimated TOON tokens, and savings for representative payloads
- TOON output does not alter field projection, ranking, redaction, provenance, degradation status, or recommended actions

title: Add migration documentation and command deprecation map for users and agents
description:
## Background

Some command behavior will change: outputs that used to contain mock reasoning will become degraded, evidence-only, or skill handoff guidance. Agents and humans need a clear migration map that preserves workflows rather than making commands feel broken.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Document the before/after split by command family. For each changed command, explain what the Rust CLI now computes, which project-local skill handles reasoning, and what to run next when output is degraded.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Documentation includes command-family before/after table, command-boundary matrix link, README workflow parity matrix, side-effect/mutation table, skill handoff table, degraded-output examples, and examples using `ee` mechanical output plus skill interpretation.
- Docs explicitly say no features were dropped; they were split by responsibility into mechanical CLI behavior, project-local skills, or honest degraded/unavailable states.
- Docs cover core context/search/why, baseline infrastructure commands, curation/review/rules, diagnostics/jobs, lab, causal, learn, procedure, preflight/tripwire, rehearse, claims/certificates, economy, situation/plan, recorder, memory/status, and skill workflows.
- Docs include repair commands and user-facing guidance for what to run next when output is degraded.
- Docs reference the mechanical-boundary ADR, command inventory, command coverage matrix, fixture corpus, side-effect contract, runtime/cancellation contract, redacted evidence handoff, and project-local `skills/` directory.
- Unit/docs tests validate deprecation-map structure, command references, workflow IDs, matrix-row links, skill paths, degraded code examples, and no stale references to removed/renamed commands.
- Link/check tests or docs e2e verify referenced command names, workflow IDs, matrix rows, and skill paths exist.
- E2E docs scripts exercise representative examples and log docs path, checked anchors, command examples exercised, skill paths, workflow rows, stdout/stderr paths, schema/golden result, and first-failure diagnosis.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: docs must map each changed workflow to one of three targets: Rust mechanical behavior, project-local skill workflow, or honest degraded/unavailable state. I accepted the bv dependency suggestion to add 3c93 -> twsq so the migration map waits for the no-fake-reasoning contract suite.

comments.author: MistySalmon

Verification via rch: cargo test --lib steward::tests::score_decay passed 3/3; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check passed; forbidden-dependency audit returned ok; ubs on src/steward/mod.rs and src/db/mod.rs exited 0 with no critical issues. Full cargo test was also run through rch and still fails on 9 unrelated pre-existing tests: cli fields output variants, supported schema list, redaction detector api key/home path, progress optional field serialization, and pack quality metric count. Agent Mail inbox/list remain unavailable due malformed mailbox DB, so this completion is mirrored in Beads.

title: EE-TST-005: Implement advanced subsystem e2e scripts for recorder, preflight, procedures, economy, learning, and causal credit
description:
Add logged e2e coverage for the advanced EE capabilities that carry the most user trust risk.

Background:
Recorder traces, preflight warnings, distilled procedures, memory economics, active learning, and causal credit can change what an agent decides to do. These features need e2e evidence that they are conservative, explainable, deterministic on fixtures, and safe under uncertainty.

Scope:
- Recorder start/event/finish/tail and replay/reconstruct scenarios.
- Preflight and tripwire checks over task and event fixtures, including false-alarm closure.
- Procedure propose/show/verify/export/promote-dry-run scenarios, including drift/failure.
- Economy report/score/budget/prune-plan dry-run scenarios, including sparse evidence and tail-risk reserve.
- Learning agenda/experiment/observe scenarios, including negative and inconclusive evidence.
- Causal trace/estimate/compare/promote-plan dry-run scenarios, including confounding and underpowered estimates.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- Keep advanced e2e scripts scenario-oriented and fixture-driven. A future agent should be able to run just `recorder`, `preflight`, `procedure`, `economy`, `learning`, or `causal`.
- Treat every advanced command as potentially safety-sensitive. Tests should assert dry-run defaults, review recommendations, degraded honesty, and no hidden mutation.
- Reuse the golden/schema contract runner rather than duplicating validation logic.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- Each advanced subsystem has at least one real-binary e2e scenario with deterministic local fixtures and detailed logs.
- E2E tests prove dry-run/non-mutating behavior for promotion, pruning, experiment, causal, and import flows before any mutating variant is trusted.
- Failure logs identify which evidence IDs, fixture families, schema paths, redaction classes, or degradation codes caused the failure.
- Advanced readiness gates depend on these scripts and cannot close on unit tests alone.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
