title: EE-092: Implement `ee agent detect --json`
description:
Source plan item: EE-092 from the `Agent Detection` backlog.

Task: Implement `ee agent detect --json`

Background and intent:
Use franken-agent-detection accretively to inventory local agent tools and source roots without replacing CASS as the primary raw history source.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-017, EE-090, EE-091. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Command output must use the stable `ee.response.v1` envelope, preserve stdout/stderr isolation, and provide useful degraded-state metadata.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Implemented conservative memory health scoring on the status memory-health model and renderers. Added score components for active ratio, freshness, confidence, provenance, and tombstone penalty; missing/invalid evidence remains conservative and yields no health score for empty memory. Updated JSON/TOON status goldens and smoke assertions. Verification: rustfmt passed on touched Rust files; git diff --check passed on touched paths; RCH remote cargo test memory_health --all-targets passed; RCH remote cargo test status_json --all-targets passed; RCH remote cargo check --all-targets passed; RCH remote cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed. Full cargo fmt --check remains blocked by unrelated existing drift in src/cli/mod.rs, src/core/recorder.rs, and src/core/repro.rs.

comments.author: MistySalmon
comments.text: Starting EE-287 after bv/br ready showed it as the only concrete ready non-epic task. Agent Mail inbox/list_agents still fail due shared SQLite corruption, so coordination is mirrored here. Reserved src/config/workspace.rs, src/config/path.rs, src/config/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/core/status.rs, tests/contracts.rs, and tests/smoke.rs via Agent Mail reservation IDs 3999-4005. First pass: inspect workspace resolution behavior, add narrow ambiguity diagnostics and executable tests without disturbing unrelated dirty work.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Completed EE-287 workspace ambiguity diagnostics. Implemented read-only workspace diagnostics in src/config/workspace.rs for explicit-vs-EE_WORKSPACE conflicts, selected-vs-discovered workspace mismatch, and nested .ee marker ancestry. Status now carries workspace selection metadata and diagnostics when --workspace is present, and JSON/human renderers expose it without stderr noise. Added real-binary smoke coverage for nested workspace ambiguity and fixed the status CLI to honor --fields for JSON output (the pre-existing field-filter renderer/tests already required this). Verification through rch: cargo fmt --check passed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo test diagnostics_report passed; cargo test fields_ passed; cargo test status_workspace_reports_ambiguity_diagnostics passed; exact forbidden-dependency audit passed; UBS on touched files exited 0 with 0 critical findings. Full rch exec -- cargo test currently fails outside this bead with 5 residual library failures tracked as eidetic_engine_cli-17ow.

title: Add README workflow parity matrix so no user-facing functionality is lost
description:
## Background

The user explicitly warned not to oversimplify or lose features. A command-by-command matrix proves coverage, but it does not by itself prove that the user journeys advertised in the README still work after splitting responsibility between mechanical CLI commands and project-local skills.

This bead creates a user-workflow parity map so future implementers can see exactly how each README workflow survives the mechanical-boundary migration.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- A README/user-workflow parity matrix exists and covers every advertised workflow and command group in the README.
- Each workflow lists the post-migration user path, required `ee` commands, required project-local skill if any, degraded/unavailable behavior, repair command, owning bead IDs, and test/e2e coverage.
- The matrix explicitly documents that no feature was dropped; any intentionally deferred behavior has a bead ID and rationale.
- Docs and e2e tests cross-check the workflow matrix so examples do not drift from implemented command names or skill paths.
- Unit or docs tests validate matrix structure and required fields.
- E2E workflow scripts log workflow ID, commands run, skill paths if used, degraded states, artifact paths, stdout/stderr paths, schema/golden status, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 888bdc9: README workflow parity rows now cite project-local skill standards paths, and the matrix contract test validates backticked skills/ paths against known project-local skill files. Verified cargo fmt --check, git diff --check on touched files, UBS on touched files, and rch cargo test --test mechanical_boundary_inventory readme_workflow_parity_matrix_covers_advertised_surfaces passed 1/1. rch clippy --test mechanical_boundary_inventory is currently blocked outside this bead by dirty src/core/causal.rs clippy errors; blocker documented in comment 587 and Agent Mail.

comments.author: PeachGull
comments.text: Implemented docs/test tightening for README workflow parity skill paths. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; git diff --check on touched files passed; UBS on docs/mechanical-boundary-command-inventory.md and tests/mechanical_boundary_inventory.rs exited 0 with no critical findings; rch cargo test --test mechanical_boundary_inventory readme_workflow_parity_matrix_covers_advertised_surfaces passed 1/1 before artifact retrieval tail was interrupted. Follow-up rch clippy --test mechanical_boundary_inventory is blocked by unrelated dirty src/core/causal.rs compile errors at lines 1787 and 1793, reported via Agent Mail to BlueKnoll.

title: Make rehearse either execute real dry-run isolation or report unavailable
description:
## Background

Rehearsal can be useful if it executes real dry-run-safe command sequences against an isolated copy or side-path store. It is harmful if it simulates command execution and reports fake changed artifacts or hashes.

Audit anchors: `src/core/rehearse.rs` currently uses stub hashes, simulated command results, and canned promote-plan steps.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Choose one honest V1 path: either implement real isolated dry-run execution with no worktrees and no destructive file behavior, or return stable unavailable/degraded responses for run/inspect/promote-plan while preserving schema. Planning validation can remain mechanical if it only validates command specs.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- No command reports executed/succeeded unless it actually ran a supported dry-run-safe operation.
- No command reports hashes, manifests, sandbox paths, or changed artifacts unless they exist or are explicitly marked planned.
- Rehearsal never uses git worktrees and never deletes files.
- Unsupported commands are reported as non-rehearsable with explicit reasons.
- Unit tests cover command spec validation, unsupported commands, no worktree policy, dry-run-only mode, and unavailable reports.
- E2E scripts log command specs, side-path location if created, artifacts, mutation posture, stdout/stderr files, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: rehearse explicitly reports unavailable per adb66e7

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: rehearse must either run a real supported dry-run in isolated side-path conditions or return stable unavailable/degraded reports. Promotion review and interpretation route to skill 0apr. No command may report executed changes, hashes, manifests, or artifact paths that do not exist.

title: Gate 17: Memory Flight Recorder Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE relies on recorder traces for counterfactual replay, preflight scoring, or release claims.

Create recorder contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/recorder_event_spine.rs
tests/golden/recorder/start_run.json
tests/golden/recorder/append_command_failed.json
tests/golden/recorder/append_redacted_secret.json
tests/golden/recorder/finish_run.json
tests/golden/recorder/import_dry_run.json
tests/golden/recorder/reconstruct_episode.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee recorder start --json` creates a run with workspace, task hash, agent identity, harness, and repository fingerprint
- `ee recorder event --json` appends exactly one event with monotonic sequence, payload hash, schema, event kind, and redaction status
- corrections and redactions are represented as new events, not in-place rewrites
- oversize, unsupported, or unredactable events return stable recorder degradation/error codes
- dry-run imports show event mapping, redaction classes, skipped events, and cursor plan without writing events
- recorder traces can reconstruct a task episode that links to context packs, preflights, outcomes, and tripwires where IDs exist
- event payloads are evidence, not instructions, and context renderers label them accordingly
- disabling the recorder does not break explicit `remember`, `search`, `context`, `outcome`, `preflight`, or `lab` commands
- recorder outputs validate against exported schemas and preserve stdout/stderr isolation

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design: Implement or verify the exact tests, fixtures, schemas, and acceptance criteria named in the gate text. If the gate cannot pass because an upstream dependency is not ready, record an explicit br comment with the blocker and degraded/deferred posture.
acceptance_criteria:

The gate-specific contract/golden/evaluation tests pass; stdout/stderr and schema behavior are verified where relevant; any claimed feature has evidence linked in comments or the close reason.

- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.

title: Gate 8: Franken Agent Detection Contract
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
Create detection fixtures before `ee agent detect` becomes part of the default binary:

```text
tests/contracts/franken_agent_detection_default.rs
tests/fixtures/agent-detect/codex/
tests/fixtures/agent-detect/claude/
tests/golden/agent-detect/detected.json
tests/golden/agent-detect/include_undetected.json
tests/golden/agent-detect/unknown_connector.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `franken-agent-detection` default features compile through `ee-agent-detect`
- default detection does not include connector parsers or SQLite-backed history readers
- default feature tree has no Tokio, Hyper, Axum, Tower, Reqwest, async-std, smol, `rusqlite`, SQLx, Diesel, SeaORM, or `petgraph`
- `root_overrides` produce deterministic detected and undetected reports
- alias normalization is stable for `codex-cli`, `claude-code`, `gemini-cli`, and `copilot-cli`
- `ee agent detect --json` preserves upstream `format_version` while wrapping it in `ee.response.v1`
- `ee status --json` reports agent inventory without making CASS import mandatory
- connector-backed features are disabled until separate direct-import fixtures exist

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

title: Reconcile existing roadmap beads with the mechanical-boundary split
description:
## Background

Existing beads and epics contain valuable functionality, but some titles and acceptance criteria assume the CLI will perform judgment-heavy work. Rather than deleting or weakening those beads, this task revises them in plan space so the same feature value is delivered through either mechanical CLI code or project-local skills.

This is where future self prevents duplicate or contradictory work across existing epics such as Counterfactual Memory Lab, Causal Memory Credit, Active Learning, Procedure Distillation, Situation Model, Preflight/Tripwires, and Agent-Native UX.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Use only `br` to update bead descriptions/comments/dependencies. Do not silently close or delete existing beads. For each affected existing bead, add a comment or revise the description to identify the target split: what remains in Rust, what moves to `skills/`, what becomes degraded until real evidence exists, and what tests must prove.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- All open/in-progress beads whose title or acceptance criteria imply reasoning, planning, causal interpretation, procedure distillation, task framing, fake outputs, side effects, runtime behavior, fixture use, or user workflow continuity are reviewed.
- Relevant existing beads are updated or commented with a mechanical-boundary note and links to the new child bead(s).
- Existing roadmap beads are checked against the command-boundary matrix, README workflow parity matrix, side-effect contract, runtime/cancellation contract, fixture corpus, skill handoff contract, and e2e logging contract.
- The review explicitly rejects closing any affected implementation or skill bead that lacks local unit/static tests, real-binary or skill e2e coverage, stdout/stderr isolation checks, and detailed first-failure logging appropriate to that bead.
- No existing functionality is dropped without an explicit follow-up bead and rationale.
- `br dep cycles` remains empty after dependency updates.
- `bv --robot-insights`, `bv --robot-triage`, and relevant `bv --robot-suggest` output are run and summarized in a closing comment.
- The final comment lists every existing bead touched, every relevant `bv --robot-suggest` item accepted/rejected, any intentional no-test exception for this plan-space bead, and why.
close_reason: Reconciled open roadmap beads with mechanical-boundary split; comments and 3c93->twsq dependency recorded; br/bv checks clean.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Final reconciliation summary for eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2. Touched/commented beads: lkw, zf1p, 5cwv, lp4p, tzmg, ynzg, twsq, gcru, 3c93, s38h, 5g6d, evah, nd65, v76q, 0hjw, 3www, ibuq, oq6h, orz6, 0apr, 1ki4, ai6, i7h, 4tr, gkn, 8mq, e3n, swx, kz1, 7g8, 7pq, 1z7, 7x5, 999, 89t, pnc, dc0w, lp4p.2, kz1.2, swx.2, swx.3, 7pq.1, 9b3j, li8g, 9eg, f01, y6q, asc, uhyw, 1ba, 1kz, mf9, i2h, 3lm, ae1, lmr. Each comment identifies what stays in Rust, what moves to project-local skills, what degrades until evidence exists, or what test/e2e logging must prove. Dependency update accepted: added 3c93 -> twsq so migration docs wait for the no-fake-reasoning contract suite. bv and graph checks: br dep cycles returned count 0; bv --robot-insights data_hash 1c272890d10856bf computed PageRank, betweenness, eigenvector, HITS, critical, cycles, k-core, articulation, and slack; bv --robot-triage data_hash 1c272890d10856bf reported 56 open, 43 actionable, 13 blocked, 6 in_progress, with top picks tzmg, lp4p, swx. Relevant bv --robot-suggest items reviewed: accepted the earlier 3c93 -> twsq dependency because docs must wait for fake-output coverage. Rejected current 3c93 -> 0apr and 0apr -> ibuq as label/template similarity that would serialize independent peer skills behind shared contracts. Rejected 0apr/0hjw label suggestions such as ee-plan, cat-testing-e2e, privacy, e2e, and epic-validation-required because existing skill/test labels are sufficient and epic labels would be misleading. Rejected broad duplicate and missing-dependency suggestions caused by generated roadmap boilerplate unless they name a concrete command-boundary dependency. No functionality dropped: affected work is preserved as Rust mechanical behavior, project-local skills, or explicit degraded/unavailable states. Intentional no-test exception: this bead is plan-space Beads hygiene only with no Rust or docs source edits; verification is br/bv plus the JSONL sync and commit, not cargo/rch.

comments.text: Post-close verification for eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2 after final sync: br dep cycles still count 0. bv --robot-triage data_hash 557b3f2cc16ea742 reports 54 open, 42 actionable, 12 blocked, 5 in_progress; top picks are tzmg, lp4p, swx. bv --robot-insights data_hash 557b3f2cc16ea742 computed all listed metrics including Cycles. bv --robot-suggest still returns 50 high-confidence suggestions; current relevant suggestions such as 0apr -> orz6, 0apr -> oq6h, 0hjw -> 0apr, and 0apr -> dc0w are rejected as peer-skill/template similarity rather than concrete command-boundary dependencies. Current 0apr/0hjw label suggestions are rejected for the same reason recorded in the final summary.

title: EE-DIST-004: Add install/update/release e2e scenarios with detailed logs
description:
Add install, update, and release-artifact e2e scenarios with detailed failure logging.

Background:
Distribution workflows fail in messy environments: stale PATH entries, bad permissions, unsupported targets, offline update checks, partial downloads, checksum mismatches, and duplicate binaries. These failures need the same agent-readable evidence discipline as EE memory workflows.

Scope:
- Host-target release package smoke test.
- Temporary HOME/PATH install-check scenarios.
- Dry-run install/update scenarios over fake/local release manifests.
- Refusal scenarios for checksum mismatch, unsupported target, unsafe overwrite, duplicate PATH binaries, and offline/degraded update posture.
- Artifact retention and first-failure summaries suitable for future agents.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive tests and logging requirements.
design: Build the e2e suite around temporary roots and fake manifests first; only later release channels should add live network checks. Failure logs should match the project-wide artifact policy.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- A real-binary e2e script or CI target runs distribution scenarios in isolated temp roots without modifying real HOME, PATH, shell profiles, install locations, or network state.
- Scenarios cover host-target packaging/manifest verification, install check, dry-run install plan, dry-run update plan, already-current idempotency, duplicate binary warning, checksum mismatch refusal, unsupported target refusal, permission denied, and offline/degraded update posture.
- Failure artifacts include enough detail for a future agent to reproduce the failing install/update posture without reading the original plan.
- CI/local verification knows how to include or skip distribution scenarios with explicit `skipped_not_configured` or `skipped_no_release_channel` codes before public release channels exist.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add unit helpers for temporary install roots, fake release manifests, fake binary shims, PATH construction, and state-hash comparisons.
- Add real-binary e2e tests for all scenarios above, validating stdout schemas/goldens and stderr/artifact diagnostics.
- Add adversarial/property tests for path traversal in artifact names, malicious archive paths, invalid checksums, weird Unicode/control characters in install paths, duplicate target IDs, huge manifests, and symlinked install roots.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, temp HOME/PATH/bin roots, sanitized environment, release manifest path/hash, binary path, source-before/source-after hashes, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden status, redaction status, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; progress, skip reasons, unsafe-refusal details, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
notes: Distribution cluster task 4: prove install/update behavior under real-binary scenarios and refusal modes.
close_reason: Completed in commit bd4559c: added 8 e2e scenarios for install/update/release workflows including idempotency, path traversal, unicode, duplicate targets, symlinks, huge manifests, and empty manifests

title: Create preflight risk-review skill that consumes ee evidence matches
description:
## Background

Risk review and ask-now prompts are useful, but they require contextual judgment. The CLI should mechanically match rules/tripwires/evidence; this skill should turn those matches into task-specific questions and verification plans for the agent.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should consume `ee preflight run --json`, `ee tripwire list/check --json`, dependency audits, and project rules. It should clearly separate evidence-backed warnings from agent-generated questions.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/preflight-risk-review/` or equivalent.
- Workflow gates on evidence quality and marks heuristic questions as agent-generated.
- Output includes risk summary, evidence-backed tripwires, ask-now questions, must-verify checks, stop conditions, and follow-up `ee` commands.
- Skill handles no-evidence/degraded outputs by asking for user confirmation rather than claiming risk certainty.
- Includes templates for preflight brief and tripwire review.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, evidence-quality gates, template fields, degraded/no-evidence refusal behavior, destructive-command escalation wording, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill fixture covers low-risk, destructive-command, migration, production/deploy, no-evidence, redacted evidence, degraded tripwire output, and malformed evidence cases with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, evidence bundle path/hash, tripwire IDs, redaction/degraded status, generated question count, stop-condition count, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Implemented preflight risk-review skill per 13970b3

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: this skill owns qualitative preflight risk review over mechanical preflight/tripwire evidence. Rust may list/check/close explicit records; the skill may interpret risk only with evidence gates, redaction handling, degraded behavior, and logged skill e2e fixtures.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented by SilentMountain. Adds src/models/id.rs with typed time-ordered IDs and 9 domain markers.

API surface:
- trait IdKind { const PREFIX: &str } (zero-sized markers, 'static lifetime)
- struct Id<K: IdKind> { inner: Uuid (v7 internally), _phantom }
- Id::now() / Id::from_uuid(uuid) / Id::into_uuid() / Id::as_uuid()
- impls: Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Display, Debug, FromStr
- ParseIdError { MissingSeparator, WrongPrefix, WrongPayloadLength, InvalidCharacter, PayloadOverflow }
- Domain aliases: MemoryId (mem_), WorkspaceId (wsp_), RuleId (rule_), PackId (pack_), SessionId (sess_), EvidenceId (ev_), AuditId (audit_), CandidateId (cand_), BackupId (bk_)

Format:
- <prefix>_<26-char-Crockford-Base32>
- Crockford alphabet: 0-9 + A-Z minus I,L,O,U
- Lowercase payloads accepted; uppercase emitted on display
- Lexicographic order matches chronological order via UUID v7 timestamp prefix

Determinism:
- Generation uses uuid::Uuid::now_v7 (48-bit ms timestamp + 74-bit random + version/variant bits)
- Equality and hash compare only the inner Uuid; the marker type carries no runtime data
- Re-parsing the Display form round-trips the underlying bytes (test exercises all 256 byte values)

Cargo.toml: added uuid = { version = '1', features = ['v7'] }.

14 unit tests cover round-trip per kind, every error variant (wrong prefix, missing separator, wrong length, invalid character, overflow, lowercase accept), ordering, debug format, hash/equality, encoder/decoder for the full byte range, and uniqueness in a 256-burst.

Verification (rch where compute matters):
- cargo fmt --check: clean
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean
- cargo test: 67/67 pass (58 lib + 7 forbidden_deps + 2 smoke)

title: Create procedure distillation and skill-authoring workflow skill
description:
## Background

Turning successful traces into reusable procedures requires judgment: selecting which steps matter, writing instructions, deciding what evidence is enough, and authoring a skill capsule. The CLI should store/export/verify procedure records, while this skill performs the distillation workflow around recorder and procedure evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should use recorder runs, curation events, procedure records, verification fixtures, and exports. It should never install skills automatically; it should produce render-only artifacts and review checklists.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/procedure-distillation/` or equivalent.
- Workflow requires source run IDs/evidence IDs before drafting a procedure.
- Output separates extracted facts, candidate steps, assumptions, verification plan, and render-only skill/capsule content.
- Skill requires `ee procedure verify` or explicit fixture evidence before recommending promotion.
- Includes templates for procedure draft, verification matrix, skill-capsule review, and drift review.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, source-evidence gates, template fields, promotion-refusal behavior when verification is missing, render-only export wording, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill fixture covers insufficient evidence, successful recorder-derived draft, failed verification, render-only export logging, redacted source evidence, and degraded `ee procedure` output cases with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, source run/evidence IDs, evidence bundle path/hash, verification status, redaction/degraded status, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.

title: EE-CONC-001: Define and test multi-agent advisory lock and concurrent-writer contract
description:
Define and validate the multi-agent concurrent-writer posture for the EE local store, end-to-end.

Background:
The plan locks the v1 architecture to a single FrankenSQLite file as the durable source of truth and assumes multiple coding agents on the same machine may share that file simultaneously. Multiple agents reading freely is supported, but heavy concurrent writes must be coordinated by a job lock or the optional daemon write owner. The plan calls this out as an explicit project risk: concurrency corruption.

The current bead set has strong database, audit, transaction, status, doctor, command-effect, and e2e coverage, but this bead owns the specific cross-cutting invariant: mutating EE commands must not corrupt durable memory when multiple agents or background jobs try to write at once.

Intended scope:
This bead owns the write-coordination contract for CLI mode without requiring a daemon. It should compose cleanly with the future daemon/steward write owner, but must not become the daemon implementation itself.

Public/internal surface:
- Internal `db::write_lock` or equivalent capability wraps FrankenSQLite write transactions in a process-level advisory lock over an EE-controlled lock path.
- Lock behavior has documented busy timeout, deterministic backoff, cancellation respect through Asupersync context, and stable diagnostics.
- `ee status --json` or related status/check output reports writer posture where safe: lock path class, busy timeout, last contention summary, and degraded posture.
- `ee diag concurrency --json` runs a non-destructive concurrency probe: lock acquire/release timing, read-while-write posture, second-writer low-timeout posture, and schema-valid triage output.
- Mutating commands honor the lock and surface a stable `write_lock_busy` or equivalent degraded/error code with a useful next action.

Why this matters:
Concurrency corruption is one of the fastest ways for an agent memory tool to lose user trust. Without an explicit, tested contract, later features such as steward jobs, MCP adapters, hooks, curation, import, rehearsal, support bundles, and background index refresh can accidentally open a second writer and cause silent state loss.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive unit tests, integration tests, real-binary e2e tests, platform notes, and detailed logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- Keep the primitive capability-narrowed and storage-local: one lock helper, one lock path policy, one busy/backoff policy, and one structured diagnostic model consumed by commands.
- Do not rely on FrankenSQLite concurrency alone for multi-agent writes. The advisory process-level lock is the product contract; database busy handling is a secondary safety net.
- Keep reads available where safe. The contract should distinguish read-only commands, derived-artifact writes, durable memory writes, config writes, and background/steward writes using the command-effect manifest.
- Platform behavior must be explicit. If Linux/macOS/Windows locking semantics differ, expose stable degraded/unsupported codes in tests and docs rather than pretending the behavior is identical.
- Use deterministic contention tests with child processes or helper binaries, bounded timeouts, fixed backoff, and artifact logs. Avoid sleep-heavy tests where a lock-step barrier or pipe can make the race deterministic.
- Ensure cancellation and interruption release locks. Tests should cover early return, validation error, transaction rollback, panic-safe/drop behavior where Rust permits, and process death/stale-lock recovery posture.
- Status/diagnostic output should not leak absolute sensitive paths by default; use redacted path classes plus hashes where needed.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- A versioned advisory write-lock contract exists for durable EE writes, including lock path policy, scope, busy timeout, deterministic backoff, cancellation behavior, stale-lock posture, platform support notes, and interaction with the future daemon/steward write owner.
- All implemented durable mutating command paths use the lock helper or explicitly document why they do not need it; read-only and dry-run commands remain non-mutating under the command-effect manifest.
- `ee status --json`, `ee check --json`, or an equivalent posture surface reports lock contention/degraded posture with stable codes and redacted path metadata where safe.
- `ee diag concurrency --json` or an equivalent diagnostic probe exercises read-while-write, second-writer-busy, timeout, stale-lock/dead-owner posture where practical, and returns schema-valid machine data without mutating durable state.
- Contention failures return stable error/degradation codes such as `write_lock_busy`, include retry/backoff/daemon/fix-plan next actions, and never leave partial durable writes.
- The contract composes with steward/daemon jobs, MCP adapters, hooks, rehearsal sandbox, import/index publish, backup/export, and curation flows without forking write semantics.
- Public schemas and golden fixtures cover no writer, active writer, busy timeout, read while writer active, stale/dead owner posture, unsupported platform behavior, cancellation, and redacted lock path reporting.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add unit tests for lock path derivation, redaction, timeout parsing, deterministic backoff, lock acquisition/release, stale-lock classification, platform capability detection, and error-code mapping.
- Add integration tests with temporary FrankenSQLite/SQLModel databases covering single writer, concurrent writers, reader during writer, failed validation before write, transaction rollback, cancellation, process death/stale lock, and duplicate idempotency keys under contention.
- Add real-binary e2e coverage that spawns isolated `ee` processes in a temporary workspace, holds a writer lock, runs read-only/status/diag commands, attempts a second mutating command with low timeout, validates stable `write_lock_busy` output, and proves durable state remains consistent.
- Add adversarial/property coverage for path traversal in lock path inputs, weird Unicode/control characters in workspace paths, symlinked state directories, huge timeout values, clock skew, corrupted lock metadata, and interrupted lock holder cleanup.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, process IDs where safe, lock path class/hash, timeout/backoff settings, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, state-before/state-after hashes, observed contention event, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; contention progress, process coordination traces, skipped platform notes, warnings, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- Closeout evidence must include fixture IDs, scenario IDs, manifest hashes, command-effect manifest entries, exact verification commands, platform coverage notes, and proof that this does not implement or require the daemon itself.
notes: Normalized during bead quality review after br lint reported the bead lacked an acceptance section. Preserved the original concurrency-corruption mitigation intent, expanded it into a testable contract, and wired it to storage, status, diagnostics, command-effect, e2e, and readiness-gate surfaces.
close_reason: Committed in b6d17b57 (EE-CONC-001 advisory lock)

title: EE-SCHEMA-DRIFT-001: Add unified schema-drift detection test for DB DDL, JSON contracts, manifests, and JSONL headers
description:
Add a schema-drift detection test that fails CI when DB schema, JSON output contracts, or index manifest format diverge from their declared specifications.

Background:
The OPUS plan §27.7 explicitly names tests/schema_drift.rs as a required gate: "regenerates DDL from #[derive(Model)] and diffs; must pass CI before merging." The bead set has EE-265 (invalid-version tests for JSON contracts), EE-266 (legacy import boundary), EE-267 (index manifest schema and validation), EE-268 (graph snapshot version validation), and EE-TST-003 (golden/schema contract runner). These cover individual contracts at point-of-use, but they do not collectively guarantee that the FULL set of schemas in the build can never silently drift.

A schema-drift test is the single CI gate that reads every declared schema (DB tables, public JSON envelopes, index manifests, jsonl headers, audit log entries, support bundle layout, configuration), regenerates them from source-of-truth definitions, and asserts byte-stability against committed fixtures. A drifted schema is either intentional (and the contributor updates the fixture in the same PR) or accidental (and CI catches it). Without this test, a single innocent attribute reordering can break every downstream consumer overnight.

Intended scope:
This bead owns the unified schema-drift test, the fixture layout under tests/contracts/schemas/, and the CI gate. It does not duplicate the per-feature golden tests; it composes them into a single drift-detection surface.

Public surface (test-only):
- tests/contracts/schema_drift.rs walks the registered schemas, serialises canonical examples for each, and diffs against tests/contracts/schemas/<name>.<version>.json.
- A small CLI helper `cargo xtask schema-drift --update` regenerates fixtures intentionally; it never runs in CI.
- ee schema list --json (already in the bead set as EE-032) is the runtime mirror; the test asserts the runtime listing matches the committed fixtures.

Why this matters:
Schema drift is the single failure mode that hurts every downstream consumer (agents, MCP clients, CI pipelines that parse JSON, future migration tooling). Catching it in a one-line CI assertion costs nothing; missing it costs every consumer a silent break.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the unified schema registry, drift fixtures, runtime schema-list parity, xtask update helper, negative tests, property tests, CI gate, and e2e logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- Use a single registry pattern: every schema crate exports a SCHEMAS slice ([Schema { name, version, example_factory }]) and the drift test iterates the slice. Adding a new schema means adding one slice entry and one fixture file; nothing more.
- Canonicalise JSON via serde_json::to_string with a stable key sort and consistent number formatting; no third-party canonicalizer is needed for v1.
- DB DDL snapshots come from the SQLModel migration registry. Avoid running the migration runner during the test; use the in-memory DDL emitter instead.
- The test must not require a network or a real cass binary. It must run in CI with no external dependencies.
- The xtask helper is a thin Rust binary in xtasks/ that imports the same registry. It writes fixture files atomically.
- Compose with EE-TST-003 (golden/schema contract runner) by sharing the canonicalization helpers and the fixture path conventions; do not duplicate the diff renderer.
- Compose with EE-TST-001 (testing strategy) by being included in the default `cargo test` set and the CI artifact policy.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The test source lives at tests/contracts/schema_drift.rs and is wired into the default `cargo test` set; it does not require any feature flag to run.
- The test enumerates every registered schema and asserts byte-stability against a committed fixture under tests/contracts/schemas/<name>.<version>.json. Coverage includes at minimum: DB DDL (all tables), every public JSON envelope (`ee.response.v1`, `ee.error.v1`, `ee.memory.v1`, `ee.search.v1`, `ee.context.v1`, `ee.status.v1`, `ee.why.v1`, `ee.workspace.identity.v1`), the index manifest, the graph snapshot manifest, the support bundle manifest, the jsonl header for memory.jsonl exports, and the audit log entry shape.
- A failing drift produces a readable diff (line-level) plus the exact `cargo xtask schema-drift --update` invocation a contributor must run to regenerate the fixture intentionally.
- `cargo xtask schema-drift --update` regenerates only the fixtures the contributor explicitly names; bare `--update` requires explicit confirmation and writes a one-line audit comment in the PR description template.
- The test asserts that every schema name has a matching version field; a schema without a version, or with a duplicate version, is a hard failure.
- The runtime command `ee schema list --json` is asserted to exactly match the committed fixture set: every runtime schema has a fixture, and every fixture has a runtime schema. No silent drift between the test fixtures and the runtime.

Testing and logging requirements:
- The drift test itself runs in under 200ms on the standard CI runner; budget is enforced with a tracing assertion.
- Negative tests prove that adding a field to any schema without updating the fixture fails CI; reordering fields fails CI; renaming a field fails CI; bumping a version fails CI unless the new fixture is added.
- A property test asserts that for every schema, canonical_json(deserialize(canonical_json(example))) == canonical_json(example) (round-trip byte stability).
- Adversarial: a fixture file that has been hand-edited to be invalid JSON fails the test with a parse error rather than a misleading drift diff; a fixture file that is missing fails with the exact regeneration command.
- E2E logs capture the schema name, fixture path, runtime path, diff summary, and the next-action command. Logs go to stderr; the diff itself goes to a per-schema log artifact.
- Closeout evidence cites the schema list, the fixture directory, and the CI workflow file that wires the test in.
notes: Closes Plan §27.7 named test (tests/schema_drift.rs). Catches a class of regressions (silent JSON contract drift) that single-feature golden tests cannot prevent because they only run when their feature is touched. Composes with EE-TST-003 rather than duplicating it.
close_reason: Added schema_drift.rs with 45+ registered schemas and 13 tests. Commit fd24f4f.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Polish pass normalization: preserved the unified schema-drift gate and added the description-side Acceptance Criteria handoff. Added explicit dependencies for the schema producers and validation surfaces named by the bead: response/error envelopes, error/exit model, index and graph manifests, support bundle, audit log, status schema, and the shared testing/golden infrastructure.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Implemented atomic derived-index publishing in src/core/index.rs: rebuild now builds into unique staging dirs, writes ee.index_metadata.v1 metadata with DB generation/document count, publishes by renaming staging into active index, retains previous active generation as index.previous(.N), and recovers by restoring retained generations or promoting complete staged generations without deleting stale incomplete staging. Added unit coverage for retained publish, retained restore, staged promotion, incomplete staging no-op, and status metadata parsing. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/core/index.rs passed; git diff --check -- src/core/index.rs passed; UBS src/core/index.rs exit 0; RCH ovh-a cargo test index_ --lib passed 53 tests; RCH ovh-a cargo check --all-targets passed; RCH ovh-a cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed. Caveat: cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in src/cli/mod.rs, src/core/recorder.rs, src/core/repro.rs.

close_reason: Completed EE-302 curation review lifecycle commands with audited storage, CLI output, and tests

comments.author: MistySalmon
comments.text: Starting EE-302 after BV triage selected it as the next unclaimed ready bead. Agent Mail inbox/listing remain blocked by malformed message DB, so coordination is recorded here. Initial scope: inspect current curation candidate/apply/validate commands and storage contracts, reserve the narrow edit surface, then add accept/reject/snooze/merge behavior with JSON tests and no silent mutation.

comments.author: MistySalmon
comments.text: Implementation note: current storage only has coarse candidate status, while EE-301 introduced review queue states in domain code. For EE-302 I am adding a forward migration with review_state/snoozed_until/merged_into_candidate_id so accept/reject/snooze/merge are explicit audited lifecycle transitions instead of overloading ttl_expires_at or silently mapping snooze/merge to rejection.

comments.author: MistySalmon
comments.text: Completed implementation: added durable curation review_state/snoozed_until/merged_into_candidate_id storage, migration v20, audited accept/reject/snooze/merge review transitions with dry-run/idempotency/blocking semantics, CLI wiring, JSON/human/TOON renderers, default queue hiding for future snoozes, unit coverage, and real-binary smoke coverage. Verification via rch: cargo fmt --check passed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo test passed; forbidden dependency audit returned ok. UBS on touched Rust files exited 0 with no critical findings; warning inventory was broad existing panic/perf/test-surface noise. Agent Mail inbox/list_agents remain blocked by malformed mailbox SQLite, so no mail acknowledgements were possible from this session.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: MistySalmon picked this up immediately after closing EE-081 because it is now unblocked and shares the same feedback scoring surface. Reserved src/db/mod.rs. Planned slice: pure deterministic confidence-decay helpers/tests only; no durable mutation or command surface.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Completed confidence decay as a pure deterministic scoring slice. Added helpful_decay_factor using the configured 90-day half-life and age-aware FeedbackCounts methods (confidence_adjustment_at_age, apply_to_confidence_at_age). Helpful evidence decays; harmful feedback remains fully weighted; confidence remains clamped. Updated the outcome feedback weight test to derive from the shared harmful multiplier. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, rch cargo test feedback --lib, rch cargo check --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and UBS on src/db/mod.rs + src/core/outcome.rs.

title: Gate 22: Causal Credit Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE uses causal uplift to promote, demote, route, or economically score memory artifacts.

Create causal credit contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/causal_credit.rs
tests/golden/causal/trace.json
tests/golden/causal/estimate_observed_exposure.json
tests/golden/causal/estimate_counterfactual.json
tests/golden/causal/compare_fixture.json
tests/golden/causal/promote_plan_dry_run.json
tests/golden/causal/audit_confounded.json
```

Pass criteria:

- `ee causal trace --json` reports exposures, decisions, outcomes, and missing evidence without mutating state
- `ee causal estimate --json` distinguishes correlation from plausible causal influence with evidence tiers and confidence
- `ee causal compare --json` can compare candidate and baseline artifacts through fixture, shadow, replay, or experiment evidence
- `ee causal promote-plan --dry-run --json` never promotes, demotes, retires, reroutes, or rewrites artifacts directly
- safety-critical warnings are never randomized away for evidence collection
- confounded or underpowered estimates produce review or learning-experiment recommendations instead of promotion
- causal outputs validate against exported schemas and preserve stdout/stderr isolation

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

## Acceptance Criteria

Verification: rch exec -- cargo test causal --test contracts reported 35 causal contract tests passed; rch exec -- cargo test causal --test advanced_e2e printed 21 causal E2E tests passed before a post-test RCH rsync delay, which later exited 0; direct rustfmt --check on the causal source/contract/e2e files passed; UBS on the causal file set exited 0 with no critical findings. Repo-wide cargo check was attempted with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ee_foggy_target and is currently blocked by unrelated active reserved edits in rule/outcome/cli files owned by GoldValley; those blockers were reported in Agent Mail thread compile-rule-protected-blocker and in br comment 511.

## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The behavior described by `Implement `ee preflight`, `ee preflight show`, and `ee preflight close --json` with compact field profiles` is implemented or, for spikes, conclusively reported with a clear go/no-go recommendation.

Testing and logging requirements:

- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.

title: Re-scope procedure commands to stored evidence, real verification, and render-only exports
description:
## Background

Procedures are valuable as evidence-backed reusable artifacts, but procedure distillation and step authoring require agent judgment. The CLI may store, list, verify, export, and track drift for procedures, but must not fabricate procedure details, mock fixture passes, or claim distillation from unspecified runs.

Audit anchors: `src/core/procedure.rs` currently returns example procedure details, sample lists, render-only skill capsules from mock snapshots, and verification results that pass by default.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Keep mechanical procedure records, provenance, verification against explicit eval fixtures/repro packs/claim evidence/recorder runs, drift detection from timestamps and contracts, and render-only exports. Move distillation and skill authoring to skills. Non-dry-run promote remains blocked until backed by real curation/audit persistence.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- `ee procedure show/list/export/verify/drift/promote` read real procedure records or return honest not-found/degraded reports.
- Verification never returns passed unless it executed or inspected explicit verification evidence.
- Exported skill capsules are render-only, provenance-bearing, and cannot be installed automatically by `ee`.
- Distillation from recorder runs is removed from CLI or limited to exporting raw candidate evidence for a skill.
- Unit tests cover not-found, empty DB, explicit fixture pass/fail, stale evidence, contract drift, export no-overwrite, and dry-run promotion blocking.
- E2E scripts log source procedure IDs, evidence IDs, fixture paths, mutation posture, stdout/stderr paths, schema/golden results, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Completed procedure command/core honesty slice with explicit-record/evidence-only behavior and passing fmt/unit/contract/check/clippy/UBS gates.

comments.author: CalmDuck
comments.text: CalmDuck start: reserved src/core/procedure.rs, procedure gate/drift contract tests, and .beads for a procedure-honesty slice. Scope is to remove generated sample procedures/mock verification passes from core helpers, add explicit-record/evidence helpers, and update focused tests without touching active qmu0/v76q surfaces.

comments.author: CalmDuck
comments.text: Completed q5vf procedure honesty slice: core procedure show/export/promote now require explicit procedure records or return honest not_found; list defaults to an empty persisted-record view; verify no longer fabricates passing results and reports pending/skipped unless explicit pass/fail evidence is supplied; propose is dry-run-only and no longer claims distillation. Added explicit ProcedureRecord/verification-result test helpers and focused coverage for not-found, empty list, explicit pass/fail, skipped sources, dry-run proposal/promotion, drift, and render-only exports. Verification: cargo fmt --check; RCH cargo test core::procedure::tests --lib -- --nocapture (22/22); RCH cargo test --test contracts procedure -- --nocapture (5/5); RCH cargo check --all-targets; RCH cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; ubs src/core/procedure.rs exit 0.

title: Wire tripwire list/check to persisted rules and explicit event payloads
description:
## Background

Tripwire evaluation is squarely mechanical: list stored tripwires, evaluate explicit conditions against explicit event payloads, and report halt/warn/audit decisions. The current sample-tripwire path undermines that trust.

Audit anchors: `src/core/tripwire.rs` calls `generate_sample_tripwires()` and evaluates conditions with a placeholder string check.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Replace sample generation with repository/database-backed tripwire retrieval. Define the minimum condition language for V1 as deterministic and auditable; reject unknown condition forms rather than guessing. Event payloads should be explicit JSON or typed event summaries.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- `ee tripwire list` reads persisted tripwires and supports stable filters/order. Empty state returns an empty list, not samples.
- `ee tripwire check` requires a real tripwire and explicit event/check input; unknown tripwire returns not_found with degradation.
- Condition evaluation is deterministic, documented, and unit-tested for every supported condition type.
- Unsupported conditions return `unsupported_condition` rather than passing or triggering heuristically.
- Unit tests cover filters, limits, empty state, not found, supported condition pass/fail, unsupported conditions, and feedback recording.
- E2E scripts log tripwire source, event payload hash, evaluation result, should_halt, mutation posture, schema/golden status, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Completed by CopperDuck: persisted tripwire list/check, explicit event payload evaluation, check-event logging, and Gate 16/golden coverage verified.

comments.author: CalmDuck
comments.text: Closeout recorded from CopperDuck completion mail after .beads reservation was released. qmu0 implementation removed sample tripwire behavior, added deterministic condition evaluation for explicit event payloads, wired persisted tripwire list/check behavior, persisted triggered state/check events, and updated Gate 16 contract/golden coverage. Reported verification: cargo fmt --check; jq empty on tripwire goldens; git diff --check for tripwire contract/goldens; RCH cargo test core::tripwire::tests --lib -- --nocapture (21/21); RCH cargo test --test contracts preflight_tripwires -- --nocapture (9/9); RCH cargo test db::tests::tripwire_store_lists_updates_and_logs_checks --lib -- --nocapture (1/1); RCH cargo clippy --test contracts -- -D warnings; ubs on tripwire contract/goldens exit 0.

title: Create situation framing and command-planning skill for agent harnesses
description:
## Background

Situation modeling and command planning are exactly the kind of agent-harness intelligence that should live in a skill. The CLI can expose command catalogs and context packs; the skill can frame the task, choose which `ee` commands to run, and explain the plan.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: The skill should use mechanical command catalogs, `ee context/search/status/doctor/preflight` outputs, and project instructions. It should never imply the Rust CLI understands the task; the skill is the reasoning layer.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Skill lives under `skills/situation-framing/` or equivalent.
- Workflow covers task classification, required evidence, command sequence selection, degraded-mode handling, and handoff to implementation.
- Output includes task frame, assumptions, selected `ee` commands, evidence gaps, risk checks, and next action for the agent.
- Skill does not store private chain-of-thought; any rationale trace is concise, evidence-backed, and safe to persist if later recorded.
- Includes templates for task frame and command plan.
- Unit/static tests validate required `SKILL.md` sections, command-plan template fields, evidence-command references, refusal/degraded behavior, ambiguous-task handling, no hidden chain-of-thought requests, and output section/schema checks.
- E2E skill fixture covers bug fix, feature, refactor, investigation, docs, deploy, ambiguous task, missing evidence, and degraded CLI evidence cases with detailed logs.
- E2E logs include skill path, fixture ID/hash, task input hash, referenced `ee` commands, evidence bundle path/hash, degraded/redaction status, output artifact path, required-section check, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Implemented in 70c7fc4: added skills/situation-framing/SKILL.md with task framing, ee command planning, degraded handling, redaction/privacy gates, no direct DB/no durable mutation rules, output template, and E2E logging requirements; added references/e2e-fixtures.md covering bug fix, feature, refactor, investigation, docs, deploy, ambiguous, missing evidence, and degraded CLI cases. Verification: skill-creator quick_validate passed; rch cargo test --test skill_standards every_project_skill_has_required_frontmatter_and_sections passed; UBS on new Markdown files exited 0/no recognizable-language findings. Broader cargo fmt/check blocked by pre-existing unrelated src/core/causal.rs formatting and compile errors.

description:

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- This scenario is the trust anchor for EE. It should prefer honest partial answers over brittle all-or-nothing failure.
- Repair actions should be machine-readable and dry-run-first, with no hidden mutation.
- Use local fixture toggles rather than actually breaking global tools.
acceptance_criteria:

- The scenario validates every named degradation code against schemas and confirms useful-output status plus next action.
- Doctor/fix-plan output is deterministic, non-destructive, and logged with command, artifact, and reason metadata.
- The scenario fails if EE silently drops degraded state, emits diagnostics to stdout, or claims confidence it does not have.

- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.

title: Define cross-cutting e2e logging standard for boundary migration
description:
## Background

The user explicitly requested comprehensive unit tests and e2e scripts with detailed logging, because this migration changes trust boundaries. A passing unit test is not enough; future agents need audit artifacts that show exactly what command ran, what it read, what it wrote or refused to write, and why output can be trusted.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Define a reusable e2e logging contract for this epic. It should align with existing test harness conventions rather than inventing a separate framework. Logs must be redaction-aware and stable enough for CI artifacts and local reproduction.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- A documented e2e log schema exists for boundary-migration tests.
- The schema records command, argv, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment, start/end timestamps, elapsed milliseconds, exit code, stdout path, stderr path, parsed JSON schema, golden validation result, command-boundary matrix row, fixture/evidence bundle hashes, redaction status, evidence IDs, degradation codes, side-effect/mutation summary, DB/index generation before/after, runtime budget/cancellation details where applicable, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Unit tests cover log schema serialization/deserialization, required-field validation, env redaction, stdout/stderr path recording, first-failure extraction, deterministic ordering, summary JSON generation, and reproduction command rendering.
- At least one smoke e2e proves the logger catches stdout pollution, schema mismatch, missing matrix row, unexpected mutation, and missing fixture hash where a fixture is required.
- All later command-family, baseline, skill, and workflow beads reference this logging contract in their acceptance criteria.
- The logging contract is compatible with CI artifact upload and local reproduction, and it is detailed enough for a future agent to diagnose the failure without guessing what was executed.
close_reason: Documented ee.e2e.boundary_log.v1 schema with 5 passing smoke tests (stdout pollution, schema mismatch, missing matrix row, unexpected mutation, missing fixture hash). Schema fields in docs/boundary-migration-e2e-logging.md and docs/testing-strategy.md. CI artifacts under target/ee-e2e/. Later beads cite this per Required References section.

title: EE-332: Implement JSON-to-TOON rendering from canonical `serde_json::Value` envelopes
description:
Source plan item: EE-332 from the `Output Encoding And TOON` backlog.

Task: Implement JSON-to-TOON rendering from canonical `serde_json::Value` envelopes

Background and intent:
Add optional TOON rendering as a token-saving output format while keeping JSON canonical for tests, schemas, hooks, MCP, audit, and storage. TOON must never become hidden state.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-017, EE-331. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Command output must use the stable `ee.response.v1` envelope, preserve stdout/stderr isolation, and provide useful degraded-state metadata.
- TOON is a renderer over canonical JSON, never a storage or audit format. Protocol outputs such as hooks and MCP stay JSON unless explicitly negotiated.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
- If a dependency or upstream Franken project is not ready, implement the smallest honest diagnostic/degraded path rather than faking behavior.
- Document any architectural exception with an ADR-oriented note and a verification hook.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The behavior described by `Implement JSON-to-TOON rendering from canonical `serde_json::Value` envelopes` is implemented or, for spikes, conclusively reported with a clear go/no-go recommendation.
- The implementation preserves local-first, no-Tokio, no-rusqlite, agent-native CLI behavior.
- Public command output, if any, validates against the stable response envelope and does not pollute stdout with diagnostics.
- Relevant unit, integration, golden, contract, or evaluation tests are added and named so future agents can find them.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and the relevant test subset pass after implementation.
- Any degraded or unavailable dependency state returns a stable error/degradation code with a useful next action.
- No silent durable mutation is introduced; write paths have explicit audit, dry-run, idempotency, or curation behavior as appropriate.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.
notes:
Original category: Output Encoding And TOON
Original plan ID: EE-332
Original dependency field: EE-017, EE-331
If this bead reveals that the original plan dependency was ambiguous or incomplete, add a br comment explaining the correction before implementation.
close_reason: Already implemented: render_toon_from_json() at output/mod.rs:1704 converts canonical JSON envelopes to TOON via toon::json_to_toon(). All report types have render_X_toon() wrappers. 12 tests pass covering parity, required fields, error handling, and golden fixtures.

title: Gate 13: Alien Artifact Certificate Readiness
description:
Readiness gate from the comprehensive EE plan.

Gate text and intent:
This gate is required before EE claims certified pack selection, calibrated curation, privacy budgets, or lifecycle proof artifacts in release notes.

Create certificate contract tests:

```text
tests/contracts/certificates.rs
tests/contracts/submodular_packer.rs
tests/contracts/curation_calibration.rs
tests/contracts/lifecycle_automata.rs
tests/golden/certificates/pack_selection.json
tests/golden/certificates/curation_risk.json
tests/golden/certificates/rate_distortion.json
tests/golden/certificates/tail_risk.json
tests/golden/certificates/privacy_budget.json
tests/golden/certificates/lifecycle_automaton.json
tests/golden/cards/math_pack_selection.json
tests/golden/cards/math_curation.json
```

Pass criteria:

- commands cannot emit `guarantee.status = valid` without a certificate ID
- `ee certificate verify <id> --json` detects stale payload hashes, stale schema versions, and failed assumptions
- pack selection certificates include selected items, rejected frontier, marginal gains, token costs, feasibility, and guarantee status
- submodular packer tests include sampled diminishing-returns checks and tiny exact-optimum comparisons
- curation certificates include calibration window ID, stratum, count, nonconformity score, threshold, action, and abstain reason when under-calibrated
- tail-risk fixtures fail when catastrophic warnings disappear even if average retrieval metrics improve
- privacy-budget certificates appear only on shareable aggregate outputs and never on ordinary local recall commands
- lifecycle automaton certificates cover cancellation, failed validation, interrupted publish, duplicate apply, and normal completion
- `--cards math` adds cards without changing the selected memories, curation decisions, or command exit code
- every card includes equation/scoring rule, substituted values, intuition, assumptions, and what would change the decision
- all certificate JSON validates against exported schemas and stays stdout/stderr clean

Why this matters:
Gates prevent roadmap gravity from turning aspirational features into unverified claims. Closing this bead means the implementation has executable evidence, not just code that compiles.

title: Add project-local skills e2e harness and fixtures
description:
## Background

Moving reasoning into skills preserves functionality only if those skills are testable. Skill tests should prove that the skill requests mechanical `ee` evidence first, handles degraded evidence honestly, and produces auditable outputs without pretending the CLI reasoned.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Add skill-lint and skill-workflow fixtures appropriate for local Codex skills. Where full agent execution is hard to automate, use deterministic transcript fixtures or prompt-output contract tests that verify required sections and evidence references.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Every project-local skill has a fixture or e2e contract proving required metadata, trigger conditions, gates, evidence commands, output contract, degraded handling, and redacted evidence-bundle handling.
- Skill tests consume the shared fixture corpus and the same evidence-handoff contract used by CLI e2e tests.
- Harness unit/static tests cover `SKILL.md` section validation, trigger metadata validation, required evidence command extraction, transcript fixture normalization, output-section validation, redaction/degraded refusal checks, and deterministic artifact naming.
- Tests cover counterfactual failure analysis, causal credit review, active-learning experiment planning, procedure distillation, preflight risk review, situation framing, rehearsal promotion review, claim/certificate review, and session-review memory distillation.
- Tests verify skills reference `ee` evidence command outputs and separate facts, assumptions, hypotheses, judgments, recommendations, and follow-up actions.
- Tests fail if a skill asks for unsupported hidden chain-of-thought, stores private reasoning as durable memory, scrapes raw DB files, ignores degraded evidence, ignores redaction state, or strengthens claims without provenance.
- E2E tests log skill path, command fixtures, evidence bundle hash, redaction classes, provenance IDs, trust classes, degraded codes, output artifact path, required-section check, related README workflow row, stdout/stderr artifact paths where commands are run, and first-failure diagnosis.
- The harness can run locally without network or paid APIs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: project-local skill e2e must prove skills consume explicit ee/CASS evidence bundles and label assumptions, judgments, degraded states, and redaction posture. Skill tests do not replace Rust command tests; they prove the handoff side of the split.

title: Define side-effect, idempotency, and mutation contracts for every command
description:
## Background

Mechanical does not only mean deterministic; it also means users can predict what a command may read, write, or refuse to touch. The project has strong no-deletion/no-worktree/no-silent-mutation rules. The mechanical-boundary beads mention mutation posture in many places, but there is no cross-cutting contract that forces every command to declare and test side effects.

This bead prevents a future implementation from preserving output behavior while accidentally making commands unsafe, non-idempotent, or surprising.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design:
Create a side-effect matrix for every public command and enforce it in tests. Each command should be classified as read-only, append-only, audited mutation, derived-asset rebuild, dry-run/report-only, side-path restore, or unavailable/degraded. For mutating commands, specify idempotency key, transaction boundary, audit entry, DB generation effect, derived index effect, rollback/recovery behavior, and whether a dry-run is required.

The matrix should explicitly forbid file deletion, worktree creation, silent overwrite, unlogged memory mutation, and background daemon requirements for core commands.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Every public `ee` command/subcommand has a documented side-effect classification and mutation contract linked from the command-boundary matrix.
- Mutating commands specify transaction scope, idempotency behavior, audit log entry, DB generation/index generation effects, dry-run support where appropriate, and recovery/degraded behavior.
- Backup/restore/export/import commands explicitly prove side-path/non-overwrite behavior and never delete files in tests.
- Tests fail if a command mutates durable state while declared read-only or report-only.
- Unit tests cover idempotent repeat calls, duplicate imports, failed transaction rollback, dry-run no-op, audit entry creation, derived-asset rebuild boundaries, and policy-denied mutation.
- E2E scripts log before/after DB generation, changed record IDs, audit IDs, filesystem artifacts created, forbidden filesystem operations checked, stdout/stderr paths, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Completed side-effect, idempotency, and mutation contract matrix. Evidence: docs/mechanical-boundary-command-inventory.md documents every public ee command family with class= side-effect vocabulary, mutation/idempotency posture, runtime/cancellation posture, fixtures/goldens, degraded codes, and schema expectations. Mutating classes name transaction/audit/idempotency/dry-run or recovery posture where applicable. docs/testing-strategy.md and tests/boundary_migration_logging.rs define/enforce boundary log fields including changed_record_ids, audit_ids, filesystem_artifacts_created, forbidden_filesystem_operations_checked, fixture_hashes, DB/index generations, stdout/stderr artifacts, reproduction command, and first-failure diagnosis. tests/effect_contracts.rs covers read-only no-mutation commands, append-only/duplicate-safe imports, backup/restore side-path/no-delete contracts, no-overwrite side-path artifacts, derived index rebuild boundaries, mutating command write surfaces, degraded unavailable paths, config/status/read-only contracts, and normalized CLI command path coverage. Verification via RCH: cargo test --test mechanical_boundary_inventory --test boundary_migration_logging --test effect_contracts -- --nocapture passed 7/7 + 8/8 + 21/21. Prior RCH cargo check --all-targets passed after the related changes. Full all-targets clippy remains blocked outside this bead in tests/skill_standards.rs under g9dq and was reported separately.
