title: Create project-local skills directory and standards for ee-adjacent agent workflows
description:
## Background

The correction is that skills belong in this project, not in an unrelated hedge-fund skills repo. Judgment-heavy workflows should live under project-root `skills/` so they are versioned with `ee`, can call the local `ee` CLI, and remain separate from the Rust binary.

This bead establishes the local skill directory structure and standards before individual skill beads implement workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Create `skills/` at the project root during implementation, with one self-contained folder per skill. Each skill should follow Codex skill conventions: `SKILL.md`, optional `references/`, optional `assets/`. Add a local README or index if useful. Skills must be explicit that they use agent intelligence around mechanical `ee` outputs; they must not imply the `ee` binary itself performs the reasoning.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- A project-local `skills/` directory exists in this repo with documented conventions.
- Standards require trigger conditions, evidence-gathering commands, stop/go gates, output templates, uncertainty handling, privacy/redaction handling, degraded behavior, and no unsupported claims.
- Standards explain how skills call `ee` mechanical commands and how they should respond to degraded outputs.
- Standards define the minimum test shape for every skill: static/unit checks for required `SKILL.md` sections and templates, fixture or transcript tests for refusal/degraded paths, and e2e contract coverage through the shared skill harness.
- Unit or lint tests verify skill folders have required files/sections if a skill-lint harness exists; otherwise add a small script/e2e check that fails loudly with the missing section/path.
- E2E logging records skill path, required files, parsed metadata, referenced `ee` commands, evidence bundle hashes, redaction/degraded states, output artifact path, and first missing requirement.
- The standards explicitly say skills may use agent judgment, but the Rust `ee` binary remains mechanical and any durable mutation must go back through explicit `ee` commands.
close_reason: Completed project-local skills standards with skills/README.md, ee-skill-standards SKILL.md, static skill folder checks, and lint-log contract tests. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo test --test skill_standards; cargo check --test skill_standards; cargo clippy --test skill_standards -- -D warnings; rustfmt --check tests/skill_standards.rs; ubs tests/skill_standards.rs. Full cargo clippy --all-targets remains blocked by unrelated dirty tests: agent_outcome_scenario_pack.rs if_same_then_else, gates/m1_storage_status.rs ptr_arg, contracts/recorder_event_spine.rs useless_vec.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Coordination note: while closing query-file bead I found current main dirty with this procedure-export work in progress and fixed compile blockers only, without changing the intended implementation: src/core/procedure.rs now records content_length before moving content into ProcedureExportReport, and src/cli/mod.rs now passes stderr into write_domain_error for procedure export/preflight error paths. Verified the current tree through rch: cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, focused query-file tests, and remember/context smoke path all pass. UBS still reports one generic hardcoded-secret heuristic without file/line detail, likely from existing redaction/test literals.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Follow-up stabilization on EE-413: fixed two stale procedure-export command hints introduced by the new renderer surface. src/core/procedure.rs now suggests `--export-format markdown` in the unsupported-format repair text, and src/output/mod.rs now shows `ee procedure export <id> --export-format markdown` in the human next-action hint. Verified via RCH with TMPDIR/CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp because local /tmp is full: `cargo test --lib procedure::tests::export_ -- --test-threads=1` passed; `cargo test --test smoke procedure_export_skill_capsule_json_exposes_render_only_artifact -- --test-threads=1` passed; `cargo check --all-targets` passed; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `cargo fmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` passed. Full `cargo test` was also attempted through RCH and failed in unrelated broad dirty-tree areas: fields output indicator tests, supported schema list, DB migration version expectations now seeing V015/V016, eval redaction detector tests, progress optional-field serialization, and pack quality/submodular tests. UBS ran with TMPDIR=/data/tmp and reports one locationless hardcoded-secret heuristic plus broad advisory noise; direct rg over the touched files found only test/diagnostic token literals such as TOKEN= in existing tests, not an apparent credential. Agent Mail remains unavailable due malformed mailbox DB, so using Beads for coordination.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: MistySalmon completed EE-413 implementation. Changes: procedure export now renders stable JSON manifest content, Markdown, YAML-like playbook, and render-only skill-capsule artifacts; skill capsules carry installMode=render_only warnings; output paths use create_new semantics to avoid overwrites; CLI uses --export-format for artifact selection while preserving the global --format renderer flag; JSON output is wrapped in ee.response.v1. Coverage added: core renderer tests, CLI parser/output tests, and a real-binary smoke test for skill-capsule JSON. Verification through RCH: cargo test export_ --lib -- --nocapture passed remotely on ovh-a; cargo test --test smoke procedure_export_skill_capsule_json_exposes_render_only_artifact -- --nocapture passed remotely on ovh-a; cargo fmt --check passed; cargo check --all-targets completed remotely on ovh-b with exit=0, followed by a non-fatal local artifact retrieval no-space warning; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings completed remotely on ovh-b with exit=0, after which I stopped the wrapper during stuck artifact retrieval. Additional checks: git diff --check passed. UBS on changed files exits 1 because of its generic hardcoded-secrets heuristic with no file/line detail; verbose output still gives no location, and the EE-413 diff contains no secret/password/token/credential strings, matching NobleCardinal’s earlier note that this is an existing generic heuristic. Coordination: NobleCardinal’s compile-fix comment was acknowledged; Agent Mail health works but list_agents/fetch_inbox remain blocked by malformed mailbox DB, and send_message has been intermittently busy.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Post-close coordination note: released 5 Agent Mail reservations at 2026-05-01T06:34:47Z. Completion send_message to NobleCardinal failed again with Resource is temporarily busy while mailbox recovery mode remains corrupt; completion details are therefore recorded here in Beads.

comments.author: NobleCardinal
comments.text: Additional coordination note while working d19r: cargo fmt --check later surfaced rustfmt-only changes in the active procedure/promote/export files, so I applied only the exact formatter edits in src/core/procedure.rs and src/output/mod.rs. No behavior changes intended in those hunks. The shared tree now passes `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` through RCH after these formatter edits.

title: EE-TST-002: Add unit-test conventions and module test templates
description:
Add the conventions and starter patterns future agents use when writing unit tests across EE crates.

Background:
The plan spans CLI parsing, response envelopes, models, SQLModel persistence, search ranking, graph scoring, redaction, curation, preflight, procedures, recorder events, economics, active learning, and causal credit. Without consistent unit-test conventions, coverage will become uneven and hard to audit.

Scope:
- Define inline or colocated unit-test conventions per crate/module.
- Provide examples for happy path, empty/boundary inputs, invalid input, degraded dependency state, stable error codes, idempotency, mutation guards, and deterministic ordering.
- Cover pure functions first: parsers, scoring, field projection, redaction classifiers, scope resolution, capability negotiation, output envelope construction, and graph/search combinators.

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:

- Keep examples small and idiomatic Rust; avoid introducing fixture bloat for pure unit tests.
- Unit tests should use deterministic test builders for workspace IDs, memory IDs, timestamps, hashes, capabilities, and degradation codes.
- Tests for feature-gated adapters should compile with relevant feature combinations where practical.
acceptance_criteria:

- A future agent can copy an existing unit-test pattern for each major crate boundary without inventing structure.
- Unit-test examples cover success, boundary, error, degraded, and idempotency/mutation-rule cases.
- The conventions explicitly prohibit tests that depend on network, wall-clock nondeterminism, ambient user config, or hidden global state.
- The project's normal verification command includes these unit tests.

title: EE-REMEMBER-LINK-SUGGEST-001: Return staged adjacency suggestions from `ee remember`
description: When `ee remember` stores a new memory, agents should get useful adjacent-memory suggestions without silently mutating the graph. This bead exposes staged adjacency candidates in the remember JSON envelope, backed by the autolink candidate generator and curation/review path.
design: This is a suggestion surface, not an auto-apply feature. It should return candidate edges such as `co_tag`, `related`, or future semantic-neighbor relations with relation, target memory ID, confidence, evidence summary, and next action. Durable `memory_links` rows are created only by explicit curation/apply commands or maintenance jobs that have their own audit behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- `ee remember --json` can include `suggestedLinks[]` when candidate adjacent memories are found.
- Suggestions include relation, target memory ID, score/confidence, evidence summary, source, and an explicit apply/review next action.
- No suggestion creates a durable memory_links row unless an explicit apply path is invoked.
- Output degrades cleanly if search/index/graph/autolink is unavailable.
- Tests cover no suggestions, tag co-occurrence suggestions, duplicate existing link suppression, degraded index, dry-run behavior, and stable ordering.
close_reason: Completed remember staged adjacency suggestions.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for remember-time link suggestions:

- The response should be useful but bounded: include a deterministic candidate limit, relation, target ID, score/confidence, score components, evidence snippets/hashes safe for output, source (`tag_cooccurrence`, `semantic_neighbor`, etc.), and explicit review/apply command hints.
- No durable memory_links row is created by normal `ee remember`; dry-run must not create memories or suggestions-derived state. Any future apply path must be explicit, audited, idempotent, and curation-backed.
- Suggestions should never make the primary remember write fail unless the suggestion subsystem corrupts stdout/schema output. Degrade with stable codes if index/search/graph/autolink is unavailable.
- Unit tests must cover candidate limit, sorting ties, duplicate suppression, self-link prevention, redaction of target content, degraded candidate source, dry-run no-write, and interaction with temporal validity.
- Real-binary e2e/golden tests must cover no suggestions, tag co-occurrence suggestions, existing-link suppression, degraded index, malformed candidate metadata, and stable JSON envelope fields with logged stdout/stderr artifacts.

title: EE-FOCUS-001: Add passive active-memory set and `ee focus` workflow
description:
Introduce a passive active-memory set for resumed work: a bounded, explicit set of memory IDs and a focal memory tied to workspace/task/recorder/handoff context. This ports the useful cognitive-state idea from legacy Eidetic without making EE an agent loop.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Frame this as `ee focus` or an equivalent active-memory command family. EE records and reports active context; it does not decide what the agent should think or autonomously execute a plan. Updates must be explicit or tied to recorder/handoff events with auditable rules. Context packs may read the active set as one explainable input.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Define storage/model for active memory sets with workspace ID, optional task/run ID, memory IDs, focal memory ID, capacity, timestamps, and provenance.
- Commands can show, set, add, remove, clear, and explain active memory state with stable JSON.
- Context/handoff can include active memory state with provenance and no hidden mutation.
- Capacity enforcement is deterministic and explainable.
- Tests cover empty state, capacity boundaries, missing memory IDs, stale/deleted memories, handoff inclusion, and context-pack influence explanations.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for passive active-memory/focus:

- Preserve the passive boundary: EE stores and explains focus state; it does not infer the agent's hidden attention, execute plans, or auto-select memories without an explicit command/handoff/recorder rule.
- Make scope precise: workspace ID plus optional task-frame ID, recorder run ID, handoff ID, profile, and source command should be stored so resumed work can distinguish parallel tasks in the same repo.
- Capacity behavior must be predictable: explicit focal memory, pinned memories, eviction policy or refusal behavior, invalid/stale memory handling, and explainable reasons for included/excluded focus items.
- Unit tests must cover state creation/update, capacity limits, pin/focal semantics, invalid memory IDs, stale/deleted memories, concurrent task scopes, audit/effect records, and no hidden mutation from read commands.
- E2E/golden tests must cover `focus show/set/add/remove/clear/explain`, handoff/resume inclusion, context-pack influence explanation, support-bundle visibility, and detailed logs with before/after focus state hashes and stdout/stderr artifacts.

Registered focus item/state schemas through src/models/mod.rs and src/models/schema.rs, added schema-drift contract coverage, and added tests/fixtures/golden/models/focus_schemas.json.golden.

It must not decide the agent plan. Coverage should include durable state, stale/degraded context, stdout/stderr isolation, and handoff/e2e logs.

title: EE-TASK-FRAME-001: Add durable non-executing task frame and goal stack
description:
Add a durable, passive task-frame/goal-stack model for agents that need to resume or hand off long work. The legacy Eidetic goal stack is useful, but EE must not become the autonomous agent loop. This bead records goals, subgoals, statuses, and links to recorder/handoff/context evidence without executing the plan.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Build on existing deterministic `ee plan` recipes and handoff capsules. A task frame should capture root goal, subgoals, current focus, status, blockers, linked memories, recorder runs, context packs, and handoff IDs. It should report state and suggested EE commands, never run shell commands or mutate project files on its own.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Define task-frame schemas for root goal, subgoals, statuses, evidence links, current focus, blockers, and timestamps.
- Commands can create/show/update/close task frames and subgoals with stable JSON and dry-run behavior where appropriate.
- Handoff/resume can include task-frame state without duplicating support-bundle diagnostics.
- `ee plan` can reference task-frame posture as input but remains non-executing.
- Tests cover nested subgoals, status transitions, ambiguous workspace/task scope, handoff inclusion, redaction, and no hidden execution.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Plan-space polish additions for task frames/goal stack:

- Preserve the non-executing contract in the schema and command-effect manifest: task frames may record goals, blockers, decisions, linked evidence, and suggested EE command recipes, but must never run shell commands, mutate workspace files, or become an agent loop.
- Model task frames as durable state with explicit lifecycle: draft/open/active/blocked/completed/abandoned plus timestamps, author/source, workspace, optional recorder/handoff/context/focus links, and redaction posture.
- Add conflict handling for multiple active frames in one workspace and clear ambiguity diagnostics for commands without a task ID.
- Unit tests must cover nested subgoal ordering, status-transition validity, evidence-link integrity, ambiguous task scope, redaction, audit/effect records, and no hidden execution.
- E2E/golden tests must cover create/show/update/close, subgoal operations, handoff/resume inclusion, plan recipe references, focus integration where available, support-bundle visibility, and detailed logs with state hashes and stdout/stderr artifacts.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space polish: removed the parent-child epic edge to avoid the open Agent-Native UX epic being treated as an implementation blocker by bv/br ready-work selection. Epic association is preserved with the epic-agent-native-ux/agent-native-ux labels; real technical dependencies remain explicit as blocks edges.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: task-frame/goal-stack work must stay durable and non-executing. Rust records explicit frames, statuses, links, provenance, and audit data; agents decide plans. Tests should cover empty/stale frames, idempotent updates, redaction, stable JSON, and logged resume scenarios.

title: EE-352: Add galaxy-brain math card examples to `ee why`, `ee pack show`, `ee curate show`, and `ee diag contracts`
description:
Source plan item: EE-352 from the `Alien Artifact Math And Certificates` backlog.

Task: Add galaxy-brain math card examples to `ee why`, `ee pack show`, `ee curate show`, and `ee diag contracts`

Background and intent:
Compile advanced mathematical ideas into inspectable runtime artifacts: certificates, cards, audits, loss matrices, tail-risk gates, and lifecycle ledgers rather than opaque magic.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-341, EE-342, EE-343, EE-346. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Command output must use the stable `ee.response.v1` envelope, preserve stdout/stderr isolation, and provide useful degraded-state metadata.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
- If a dependency or upstream Franken project is not ready, implement the smallest honest diagnostic/degraded path rather than faking behavior.
- Document any architectural exception with an ADR-oriented note and a verification hook.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The behavior described by `Add galaxy-brain math card examples to `ee why`, `ee pack show`, `ee curate show`, and `ee diag contracts`` is implemented or, for spikes, conclusively reported with a clear go/no-go recommendation.
- The implementation preserves local-first, no-Tokio, no-rusqlite, agent-native CLI behavior.
- Public command output, if any, validates against the stable response envelope and does not pollute stdout with diagnostics.
- Relevant unit, integration, golden, contract, or evaluation tests are added and named so future agents can find them.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and the relevant test subset pass after implementation.
- Any degraded or unavailable dependency state returns a stable error/degradation code with a useful next action.
- No silent durable mutation is introduced; write paths have explicit audit, dry-run, idempotency, or curation behavior as appropriate.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.

Original plan ID: EE-352
Original dependency field: EE-341, EE-342, EE-343, EE-346

comments.text: Completed EE-207 daemon foreground mode. Added a top-level ee daemon --foreground path backed by steward DaemonForegroundOptions and run_daemon_foreground. The implemented mode is intentionally bounded/current-process only: it does not fork, does not claim background write ownership, and returns a stable unsatisfied_degraded_mode error for background daemon usage. JSON output uses the ee.response.v1 envelope with data schema ee.steward.daemon_foreground.v1, no progress/debug output on stdout, and a stable degraded note that background mode is not implemented. Added tests: 5 focused daemon unit/CLI tests for parsing, response envelope, background-mode error, configured job execution, and zero-tick validation; plus a real-binary smoke test for ee daemon --foreground --once --interval-ms 0 --job health_check --json. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib daemon passed 5/5; cargo test --test smoke daemon_foreground_once_json_real_binary_smoke passed 1/1; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check passed; forbidden-dependency audit returned ok; ubs on src/steward/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, and tests/smoke.rs exited 0 with no critical issues. Project-wide cargo test remains blocked by the same 9 unrelated failures already documented: cli fields variants, supported schema list, redaction detector api key/home path, progress optional field serialization, and pack quality metric count. Agent Mail inbox/list are still unavailable due malformed mailbox DB, so completion is mirrored here.

title: EE-DIST-005: Add agent docs and planner recipes for install/update recovery
description:
Add agent-facing install, verify, update, and recovery recipes to EE docs and planner surfaces.

Background:
Once distribution exists, agents need compact machine-readable recipes for common operational questions: how do I verify the binary, how do I know whether it is stale, how do I update safely, how do I pin a version, and what evidence should I attach to a support bundle when install/update fails?

Scope:
- Extend agent-docs recipes and examples for install/check/update/pin/diagnose.
- Expose planner recipes for installation verification and update planning.
- Include support-bundle and handoff references for binary provenance and failed-update diagnostics.
- Keep docs machine-readable and schema-tested, not prose-only.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive tests and logging requirements.
design: Reuse agent-docs recipes and the goal planner. Keep installation guidance exact, machine-readable, and tied to implemented command/effect metadata.
acceptance_criteria:

Testing and logging requirements:

- Add golden/schema tests for agent-docs and planner recipe outputs.
- Add e2e coverage that invokes the docs/planner distribution recipes and validates that generated commands match implemented install/update/version surfaces.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd, sanitized environment, recipe ID, schema/golden status, stdout/stderr artifact paths, redaction status, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; docs-generation diagnostics and skipped release-channel explanations go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
notes: Distribution cluster task 5: make install/update operations discoverable and recoverable for agents.
close_reason: Added 7 install/update/recovery recipes with failure branches. Commit ad10e2c.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented by SilentMountain. Adds src/models/provenance.rs with strict round-trippable URI parser/renderer.

API surface:
- enum ProvenanceUri { CassSession{session, span}, File{path, span}, EeMemory(MemoryId), Web{url}, AgentMail{thread, message} }
- struct LineSpan { start: u64, end: Option<u64> } with single() / range() constructors that reject 0 and inverted ranges
- ProvenanceUri::scheme() returns canonical scheme name
- Display + FromStr for ProvenanceUri (round-trippable)
- enum ProvenanceUriError { Empty, MissingScheme, UnknownScheme, EmptyBody, InvalidWebBody, InvalidAgentMail, InvalidFragment, ZeroLineNumber, InvertedLineRange, InvalidMemoryId{source:ParseIdError} }

Schemes supported (v1):
- cass-session://<id>[#L<n>] / [#L<n>-<m>]
- file://<path>[#L<n>] / [#L<n>-<m>]
- ee-mem://<MemoryId>  (full validation through EE-060 MemoryId::from_str — wrong prefix, bad chars, overflow all rejected)
- https://... / http://... (rejects whitespace + control chars in body)
- agent-mail://<thread>[/<message>]

Discipline:
- Round-trip contract: ProvenanceUri::from_str(s)?.to_string() == canonical(s).
- LineSpan with end == start collapses on display to '#L<n>'.
- Input is trimmed of surrounding ASCII whitespace before parsing.
- ParseIdError from EE-060 is wrapped as ProvenanceUriError::InvalidMemoryId{source}; std::error::Error::source returns the inner error.
- No new deps; uses uuid only via the existing MemoryId surface.

21 unit tests cover round-trip per scheme, every error variant (empty input, missing scheme, unknown scheme, empty body per scheme, invalid web body, invalid agent-mail, every invalid-fragment shape including missing 'L', empty number, range-without-end, range-without-start, non-numeric), zero/inverted line ranges, equal-endpoint collapse, scheme() canonicalization, and ee-mem rejection of wrong-prefix IDs.

Verification (rch where compute matters):
- cargo fmt --check: clean
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean
- cargo test: 105/105 pass (95 lib + 7 forbidden_deps + 2 smoke + 1 doctest harness)

title: EE-TST-009: Add command effect manifest and no-mutation contract suite
description:
Define a first-class command effect manifest and contract suite so every public EE command is explicit about what it can read, write, cache, mutate, or refuse to do.

Background:
The bead graph already requires read-only behavior, dry-run behavior, idempotency, audit records, and no hidden mutation in many individual places. That is good but still too distributed for a CLI whose primary users are agents. Agents need to know before invoking a command whether it is safe to call in the middle of a task, whether it can write durable memory, whether it can rebuild derived indexes, whether it can touch workspace files, and how to verify that dry-run actually did not mutate anything.

Scope:
- Define a command-effect taxonomy for all CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, and maintenance surfaces.
- Add a generated or hand-maintained manifest that maps each public command and subcommand to its default effect, dry-run effect, allowed write surfaces, idempotency behavior, audit behavior, and external I/O posture.
- Add a no-mutation contract suite that snapshots isolated workspace state before and after commands and fails on undeclared durable writes.
- Integrate effect metadata into public schemas, capabilities, test artifacts, and closure dossiers.
- Make derived-cache or index writes explicit rather than pretending they are the same as pure read-only operations.

Why this improves user outcomes:
EE is meant to be called by agents inside active coding sessions. The safest and most ergonomic interface is one where agents can mechanically choose safe commands, prove that read-only commands stayed read-only, and trust that mutating commands advertise dry-run, idempotency, audit, and repair behavior. This reduces accidental memory corruption, hidden cache surprises, MCP adapter divergence, and user mistrust.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive unit tests, e2e tests, contract fixtures, and logging requirements.
design:
Recommended design:
- Define stable effect classes such as `read_only`, `derived_artifact_write`, `durable_memory_write`, `workspace_file_write`, `config_write`, `external_io`, and `disabled_until_safe`. The exact enum names can change during implementation, but the distinction between pure reads, declared derived writes, and durable user-visible mutation must remain.
- Each command manifest entry should include command path, schema version, default effect, dry-run effect, allowed database tables, allowed derived artifact paths, allowed workspace file paths, external I/O posture, idempotency-key requirement, audit requirement, confirmation/ask-before-acting behavior, and degraded fallback behavior.
- Read-only commands should be tested by state snapshots over the database, index/cache roots, config home, and workspace files. Derived writes are acceptable only when declared, isolated, and either disabled under strict mode or reported as derived artifacts.
- Mutating commands should be contract-tested for dry-run, idempotency replay, audit record creation, duplicate apply handling, and failure before partial durable writes where possible.
- MCP tools should use the same manifest, with read-only/idempotent annotations derived from the CLI service contract rather than handwritten adapter claims.
- Logs should include `effect.expected`, `effect.observed`, snapshot hashes, changed paths/tables, strict-mode status, and first undeclared mutation if the test fails.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- A versioned command-effect manifest exists for public CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, and maintenance commands as they are implemented.
- Each manifest entry declares default effect, dry-run effect, allowed write surfaces, idempotency/audit requirements, external I/O posture, degraded fallback behavior, and whether strict no-derived-write mode applies.
- Public capability/schema output exposes enough effect metadata for agents to choose safe commands without reading source code.
- A no-mutation contract suite snapshots isolated workspace state before and after read-only and dry-run commands and fails on undeclared database, index/cache, config, or workspace-file changes.
- Derived cache/index writes are either declared and logged or disabled in strict no-mutation tests; they cannot silently pass as pure read-only behavior.
- Mutating commands have contract coverage for dry-run, idempotency replay, audit creation, duplicate apply, and failure handling before partial durable writes wherever the command supports mutation.
- MCP read-only/idempotent annotations are verified against the same manifest and cannot diverge from CLI service behavior.
- E2E and closure dossier artifacts include effect expectation, observed state delta, snapshot hash paths, and first undeclared mutation diagnosis.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, effect expectation, observed state delta, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, debugging context, effect-diff summaries, and snapshot paths must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.
notes: Added during repeated bead quality review. This makes scattered read-only, dry-run, idempotency, and no-hidden-mutation requirements enforceable through one manifest and one contract suite.
close_reason: Implemented effect manifest with EffectClass, CommandEffect, EffectManifest, 17 unit tests, and no-mutation integration tests

title: Add no-fake-reasoning contract suite for all migrated commands
description:
## Background

This is the cross-cutting safety net for the migration. The core problem is not just code shape; it is that an agent might trust output that looks like it came from evidence when it actually came from canned samples or heuristic judgment.

This bead adds contracts that fail loudly when a migrated command claims unsupported reasoning, success, verification, uplift, procedure validity, risk assessment, or health.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Build on the command inventory, degraded-honesty contracts, and e2e logging standard. Prefer real-binary contract tests that exercise public command paths in isolated workspaces. Static scans may supplement but must not be the only protection.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Contract suite covers core retrieval/packing/explanation (`search`, `context`, `pack`, `why`, graph witness/explain paths), baseline infrastructure commands, recorder, tripwire, preflight, procedure, causal, lab, learn, review/curate/rule lifecycle, diagnostics/jobs/daemon/adapters, memory revise/status, economy, situation/plan, rehearse, and claims/certificates.
- Unit/contract tests cover forbidden-output scanners, fixture-mode detection, provenance validators, schema validators, degradation-code validators, stdout/stderr isolation checks, side-effect declaration checks, and stable repair-command rendering.
- Tests fail when normal command output contains sample/mock/stub/demo data or reports success, health, verification, replay, causal uplift, procedure validity, risk assessment, curation maturity, graph explanation, certificate validity, mutation, or pack selection without evidence.
- Tests explicitly allow deterministic fixtures only in fixture/eval/test modes and verify that fixture mode is marked in output.
- Tests validate stable JSON schemas, degradation codes, stdout/stderr isolation, provenance completeness, score-component traceability, redaction status, side-effect classification, idempotency behavior, runtime budget/cancellation behavior where applicable, and repair-command stability.
- The suite cross-checks the command-boundary matrix so every public command is either covered directly, intentionally out of scope with rationale, or marked feature-gated/degraded.
- E2E scripts run representative real-binary cases for fake-output rejection and honest degradation, not just unit-level scanners.
- Logs include command, workspace, evidence source summary, fixture hashes, parsed JSON schema, forbidden-output scan result, degradation code set, side-effect summary, runtime outcome, provenance IDs, redaction state, stdout/stderr paths, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Contract output is suitable for CI artifacts and local reproduction.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: this is the cross-command enforcement bead. It should cover every migrated command family and skill handoff for fake success, unsupported reasoning, fixture-mode leakage, provenance gaps, stdout/stderr mixing, undeclared side effects, and unstable repair commands before docs or epic closure claim the split is safe.

title: Preserve context/search/why as deterministic explainable retrieval, not agent reasoning
description:
## Background

The headline `ee` workflow is still `context -> search -> why -> pack`. These surfaces are allowed to compute ranking, packing, graph boosts, decay, profile quotas, provenance, and score explanations, but only as deterministic math over stored memories, indexes, graph snapshots, fixtures, and explicit query inputs.

This bead prevents an overcorrection. We should not remove context packs, score explanations, counterfactual score hints, profile behavior, or graph-aware retrieval simply because their human-facing wording can sound intelligent. The fix is to make every explanation traceable to concrete score components and evidence, not to delete the feature.

Risk to avoid: a future agent could replace rich context behavior with a dumb list of memories. That would technically be mechanical but would lose the product's primary user value.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- `ee search/context/pack/why` preserve rich explainable retrieval while proving every score and explanation field is computed from persisted records, Frankensearch results, FrankenNetworkX snapshots, explicit profile config, or deterministic fixtures.
- Graph explanation commands report witness nodes/edges and metric components, not qualitative relationship stories.
- Counterfactual score hints are renamed or documented as deterministic sensitivity reports unless backed by replay evidence.
- Unit tests cover empty DB, lexical-only degradation, graph-stale degradation, deterministic tie breaks, pack-hash reproducibility, score-component math, profile quotas, and provenance completeness.
- Golden tests pin stable JSON/Markdown/TOON output for representative context/search/why paths.
- E2E scripts log query, workspace, DB/index generations, candidate IDs, score components, selected pack IDs, pack hash, degraded capabilities, stdout/stderr paths, schema/golden status, and first-failure diagnosis.
- No feature is dropped: context profiles, provenance, graph boosts, decay/confidence weighting, and why explanations remain available when their mechanical inputs exist.

comments.author: IcyRiver
comments.text: Progress from IcyRiver before SilentPine took the next graph slice: real-binary search/context/why coverage was expanded in tests/advanced_e2e.rs to assert search --explain score factors, metric explanation coverage, context pack provenance/scores/selection certificate/objective components, and why storage provenance/retrieval score inputs/deterministic formula/pack hash/relevance/utility. Verification before current shared-tree movement: focused real-binary test passed, cargo test --test advanced_e2e -- --nocapture passed 56/56, cargo check --all-targets passed, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed, cargo fmt --check passed, and UBS on tests/advanced_e2e.rs had heuristic warnings only. Current shared verification later passed again for cargo check/clippy/fmt/diff, and SilentPine is now working the remaining graph metric component evidence slice on src/graph/mod.rs.

comments.author: IcyRiver
comments.text: Tracker coordination: marked in_progress because SilentPine announced and reserved the remaining graph metric component evidence slice on src/graph/mod.rs in Agent Mail at 2026-05-04T01:00:21Z. This avoids bv offering tzmg to another agent while that work is active; IcyRiver is not editing tzmg files.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: context/search/pack/why and graph explanations remain Rust functionality when every field is deterministic and provenance-backed. Skills may decide when to ask for context or how to interpret it, but they must not replace pack assembly, score components, witness paths, degraded entries, or stable pack hashes.

title: Epic: Distribution, Installation, and Update Reliability
description:
Program epic for getting trustworthy EE binaries onto agent machines and keeping them diagnosable after installation.

Background and intent:
The current bead graph is strong on memory architecture, agent-native command contracts, testing, diagnostics, support bundles, handoff, audit, rehearsal, and planning. What is still under-owned is distribution: how a future agent installs `ee`, verifies exactly which binary it is running, updates safely, proves release artifacts match source, and captures install/update diagnostics without leaking secrets.

For a Rust CLI intended exclusively for agents, distribution is part of the product. A brilliant memory engine that is hard to install, impossible to verify, or silently stale in PATH will fail in real-world practice. This epic makes install/release/update reliability explicit while preserving the local-first core design.

Scope:
- Version and build provenance surfaces for `ee` binaries.
- Release artifact manifest, checksums/signatures, and cross-platform packaging posture.
- Agent-safe installer/update/check behavior with dry-run, idempotency, and no hidden workspace mutation.
- Real-binary install/update e2e tests with detailed logs and offline/degraded coverage.
- Agent docs and planner recipes that explain install, verify, update, pin, diagnose, and support-bundle workflows.

This epic does not require publishing before the product is ready, and it does not weaken local-first behavior. It ensures release readiness has a real implementation path and test evidence instead of being a vague final step.

## Success Criteria

Detailed epic closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as the epic's success contract, including cross-bead user validation, evidence, and logging requirements.
design: Treat distribution as an agent UX surface. Keep release/install/update logic outside the memory core, but expose enough machine-readable provenance and diagnostics that agents can trust which binary they are using and recover from stale or failed installs.
acceptance_criteria:
## Success Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- All child distribution/release beads are closed.
- A future agent can install or update `ee`, verify binary provenance, inspect release metadata, diagnose a failed install/update, and produce a supportable evidence bundle without reading the original Markdown plan.
- Release artifacts, installer behavior, update behavior, and version output have stable schemas/goldens where public JSON is emitted.
- Distribution behavior preserves the same non-interactive agent-native rules as the CLI: stdout is machine data, stderr/artifacts hold diagnostics, and mutating actions are dry-run/idempotent/auditable where applicable.
- Cross-platform packaging and install/update tests are represented in local/CI verification with clear artifact retention and first-failure diagnosis.

Epic-level user validation requirements:
- Before this epic closes, child beads exposing user/agent behavior must have concrete unit, golden/schema, e2e, replay, or scenario evidence appropriate to their risk.
- The close reason or final comment must summarize installed workflows, verification commands, artifact/log locations, degraded states covered, and intentionally postponed release channels with follow-up bead IDs.
- Do not close this epic by creating release scripts alone; close it only when install/update/provenance behavior is exercised by real-binary tests and no planned distribution functionality was silently dropped.
notes: Added during repeated bead quality review after searches found hook installer tests and release-failure fixtures but no concrete EE binary distribution/install/update ownership.

title: Create deterministic fixture corpus and golden artifacts for boundary tests
description:
## Background

The previous beads require comprehensive unit, golden, contract, and e2e coverage, but they do not yet define the shared fixture corpus those tests should use. Without a carefully designed fixture set, future implementers can accidentally write shallow tests that only prove the happy path or rely on ad hoc sample data that later leaks into production command output.

This bead creates the deterministic fixture and golden-artifact foundation for the entire mechanical-boundary migration. It is plan-space insurance against two failure modes: under-testing and fake production data.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- A deterministic fixture corpus exists for boundary migration tests, with documented fixture IDs, data hashes, provenance URIs, schema versions, redaction state, and intended command coverage.
- Fixtures cover empty, happy-path, boundary, degraded, malformed, stale, redacted, prompt-injection-like, and cross-command provenance scenarios.
- Golden artifacts exist or are planned for stable JSON, Markdown, TOON, error/degradation envelopes, e2e summary JSON, and skill evidence bundles.
- Tests fail if fixture records appear in normal non-fixture workspace output without an explicit fixture/eval marker.
- Unit/property tests cover fixture builders, stable IDs, hash reproducibility, redaction placeholders, timestamp normalization, and deterministic ordering.
- E2E fixture smoke script logs fixture name, fixture hash, workspace path, DB/index generations, schema versions, redaction classes, command matrix rows exercised, stdout/stderr artifact paths, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Completed deterministic boundary fixture corpus and smoke contract. Evidence: tests/fixtures/boundary_corpus/corpus.json now defines sorted test-only fixtures for empty, happy-path, boundary, degraded, malformed, stale, redacted, prompt-injection, and cross-command provenance scenarios with content hashes, normalized UTC timestamps, provenance URIs, schema versions, redaction/quarantine state, degraded codes, intended command coverage, fixture-mode and no-leakage flags, plus planned/existing golden artifact coverage for JSON, Markdown, TOON, error/degradation, e2e summary, and skill evidence bundle. tests/fixtures/boundary_corpus/README.md documents the corpus and smoke dossier fields. tests/boundary_fixture_corpus.rs verifies stable metadata, deterministic ordering, BLAKE3 hash reproducibility, timestamp normalization, scenario coverage, redaction/degraded/quarantine state, golden plan references, and fixture-mode e2e smoke summary logging fixture name/hash, workspace path, DB/index generations, schema versions, redaction classes, command matrix rows, stdout/stderr artifact paths, first-failure diagnosis, and no normal-output fixture leakage. Verification via RCH: cargo fmt --check passed; cargo test --test boundary_fixture_corpus -- --nocapture passed 5/5; cargo clippy --test boundary_fixture_corpus -- -D warnings passed; cargo check --all-targets passed. jq JSON validation and trailing-whitespace scan clean. Full all-targets clippy remains blocked outside this bead in tests/skill_standards.rs under g9dq and was reported separately.

title: EE-DIST-001: Add binary version and build provenance contract
description:
Define and implement stable version, build, and release provenance surfaces for the `ee` binary.

Background:
Agents need to know exactly which `ee` binary they are invoking, which source revision produced it, which feature flags and optional adapters are compiled in, which schema/API versions it supports, and whether the binary is compatible with the current workspace database. Human-readable `--version` text is not enough for automation.

Scope:
- `ee --version` remains human-friendly where appropriate.
- `ee version --json` or equivalent emits machine-readable provenance.
- Build metadata includes crate version, git commit/tag/dirty state where available, target triple, build profile, feature flags, schema/API versions, release channel, and supported workspace DB migration range.
- Provenance output is redaction-safe and does not leak paths or secrets from the build environment.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including comprehensive tests and logging requirements.
design: Use compile-time metadata and existing response/schema helpers. Keep build provenance read-only and redaction-safe; do not make it depend on network access or ambient git state at runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- `ee version --json` returns an `ee.response.v1` payload with binary version, git/source identity where available, target triple, build profile, enabled feature flags, release channel, schema/API versions, supported DB migration range, build timestamp policy, and provenance availability/degradation codes.
- Human-readable `ee --version` stays concise and never replaces the machine-readable contract.
- Version/provenance output is deterministic under fixed build metadata fixtures and redacts or omits absolute build paths, user names, tokens, and environment-specific secrets.
- Public schemas and golden fixtures cover release build, dev build, dirty source state, missing git metadata, feature-gated adapters, unsupported DB version range, and redacted build-path data.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add unit tests for build metadata normalization, version compatibility checks, feature flag reporting, redaction, and missing metadata degradation.
- Add golden/schema tests for every public version/provenance response shape.
- Add real-binary e2e coverage that invokes the compiled binary, validates `ee version --json`, checks stdout/stderr isolation, and records artifact paths and first-failure diagnosis.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden status, redaction status, binary path, target triple, feature summary, and first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; provenance diagnostics and missing metadata explanations go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
notes: Distribution cluster task 1: make the running binary self-describing for agents.
close_reason: Implemented stable ee version provenance JSON/TOON rendering, concise human --version, golden and real-binary artifact coverage, and RCH-backed focused verification. Full shared golden matrix remains blocked by unrelated check_json fixture drift documented in comments.

title: EE-144: Implement section quotas
description:
Source plan item: EE-144 from the `Context Packing` backlog.

Task: Implement section quotas

Background and intent:
Make ee context the primary product surface: small, deterministic, explainable packs with sections, quotas, redundancy control, provenance, audit hashes, and why support.

Why this matters:
This bead contributes to the overarching goal of making `ee` a dependable local memory substrate for coding agents: useful before work, auditable after work, and safe under degraded conditions. The work should be implemented in the smallest coherent slice that fully satisfies the public contract implied by the title.

Dependency context:
Original plan dependencies: EE-143. These are also represented as br dependency edges where the referenced bead exists.

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.
- Use Asupersync for async edges and cancellation/budget behavior; do not smuggle in a Tokio executor.
- Keep JSON as the canonical public contract. TOON, Markdown, diagrams, and MCP are renderers or adapters over the same domain data.
- Mutating commands need dry-run/idempotency/audit behavior unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Add focused tests proportional to risk: unit tests for domain logic, contract/golden tests for public output, and integration/evaluation tests for cross-subsystem behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Detailed closure criteria are stored in this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field. Treat those criteria as part of this bead's implementation contract, including the comprehensive testing and logging requirements.
design:
Suggested design approach:
- Command output must use the stable `ee.response.v1` envelope, preserve stdout/stderr isolation, and provide useful degraded-state metadata.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
- If a dependency or upstream Franken project is not ready, implement the smallest honest diagnostic/degraded path rather than faking behavior.
- Document any architectural exception with an ADR-oriented note and a verification hook.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

Acceptance criteria:
- The behavior described by `Implement section quotas` is implemented or, for spikes, conclusively reported with a clear go/no-go recommendation.
- The implementation preserves local-first, no-Tokio, no-rusqlite, agent-native CLI behavior.
- Public command output, if any, validates against the stable response envelope and does not pollute stdout with diagnostics.
- Relevant unit, integration, golden, contract, or evaluation tests are added and named so future agents can find them.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and the relevant test subset pass after implementation.
- Any degraded or unavailable dependency state returns a stable error/degradation code with a useful next action.
- No silent durable mutation is introduced; write paths have explicit audit, dry-run, idempotency, or curation behavior as appropriate.

Testing and logging requirements:
- Add or update unit tests for core logic introduced or changed by this bead, covering happy path, boundary/empty inputs, invalid inputs, degraded dependencies, deterministic ordering, and idempotency/mutation rules as applicable.
- If the behavior is visible through the CLI, MCP, hook, export, renderer, or evaluation output, add or extend a real-binary e2e, golden, schema, contract, replay, or evaluation fixture that exercises the public surface in an isolated local workspace.
- E2E/logged tests must capture command, cwd/workspace, sanitized environment overrides, elapsed time, exit code, stdout/stderr artifact paths, schema/golden validation status, redaction status, and a concise first-failure diagnosis.
- Stdout assertions must prove stdout contains only machine data; diagnostics, progress, and debugging context must go to stderr or explicit artifacts.
- If no executable test is appropriate for this bead, the close reason or a br comment must explain why and identify the nearest bead that provides coverage.
notes:
Original category: Context Packing
Original plan ID: EE-144
Original dependency field: EE-143
If this bead reveals that the original plan dependency was ambiguous or incomplete, add a br comment explaining the correction before implementation.
close_reason: Implemented SectionQuota and SectionQuotas types with profile-based allocation (Balanced/Compact/Thorough). 10 new tests pass covering quota logic, profile allocations, and capacity checks.

title: Add runtime cancellation and budget tests for migrated command paths
description:
## Background

The README promises cancellation-correct behavior through Asupersync: long operations respect `&Cx`, budgets, and `Outcome`, and tests use `LabRuntime`. The mechanical-boundary plan currently requires e2e coverage and deterministic output, but it does not explicitly protect cancellation, budget exhaustion, or partial-progress cleanup across migrated command families.

This matters because a deterministic command that hangs, ignores cancellation, or leaves half-written state is not trustworthy for agents.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design:
Add a cross-cutting runtime verification plan for migrated commands. Use Asupersync `LabRuntime` or equivalent deterministic runtime fixtures to cover cancellation points, budget exhaustion, scope supervision, partial-write rollback, and degradation envelopes. Prefer command-family unit tests plus a small number of real-binary e2e budget/cancellation cases rather than trying to make every command artificially long.

The goal is not to over-engineer runtime infrastructure; it is to make sure every long-running or multi-stage command declares where cancellation is observed and how users can diagnose budget/degraded outcomes.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Every migrated command family identifies whether it is synchronous/short, potentially long-running, or multi-stage, and documents budget/cancellation behavior.
- Unit/runtime tests cover the command runtime-classifier, budget/deadline math, cancellation envelope construction, partial-progress cleanup policy, and deterministic `Outcome` mapping.
- Long-running or multi-stage paths have deterministic runtime tests using `LabRuntime` or a project-standard equivalent.
- Tests cover cancellation before start, cancellation mid-operation, budget exhaustion, storage/index failure after partial progress, supervised child task failure, and stable degraded/error output.
- Mutating commands prove cancellation does not leave unaudited partial durable state.
- E2E scripts include at least one real-binary budget/cancellation scenario for import/index/replay/e2e-suite style paths with detailed logs.
- Logs include budget, cancellation injection point, observed `Outcome`/exit code, records written, records rolled back or audited, stderr/stdout paths, command-boundary matrix row, side-effect class, and first-failure diagnosis.
notes:
TurquoiseDuck 2026-05-03: completed non-overlapping boundary-log runtime evidence slice in tests/boundary_migration_logging.rs while core classifier/docs files were reserved by other agents. Added required runtime log fields and validation for side-effect class, cancellation injection point, observed outcome/exit-code consistency, runtime budget shape, cancellation/timeout outcome mapping, and rollback/audit evidence for partial writes. Added accepted scenarios for cancellation before start, cancellation mid-operation, budget exhaustion, storage failure after partial progress, and supervised child failure, plus rejection cases for inconsistent envelopes. Verified via RCH: cargo test --test boundary_migration_logging -- --nocapture passed 10/10; cargo clippy --test boundary_migration_logging -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check passed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings remote command reported exit 0.

CalmDuck 2026-05-03: completed the runtime classifier/docs/manifest slice. Added RuntimeClass and CommandRuntimeContract to src/core/effect.rs, wired runtime contracts into all CommandEffect constructors, documented cancellation points/partial-progress policies/outcome mappings, and added deterministic budget math. Added effect contract coverage for runtime classes, positive budget defaults, zero-budget rejection, outcome fields, reciprocal CLI/manifest drift detection, and removed manifest-only paths reported by JadeJay: diag trust, import list, index refresh, memory show-link, memory show-links, schema show. Extended boundary logging docs and tests with runtime side-effect evidence and added a real-binary budgeted index rebuild dry-run boundary log after init plus remember seeding. Verification: RCH cargo test --test effect_contracts -- --nocapture passed 25/25; RCH cargo test --test boundary_migration_logging -- --nocapture passed 11/11; RCH cargo fmt --check passed; RCH cargo check --all-targets passed; RCH cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings remote exit 0; git diff --check on touched files passed; UBS on touched Rust files exited 0 with heuristic warnings only.

ProudBasin 2026-05-03: completed the supervised child failure runtime contract slice in tests/contracts/asupersync_cancellation.rs. Added LabRuntime coverage for a supervised child task that reports a storage-domain failure after staged progress, records rollback/audit evidence, propagates the domain-error outcome, verifies exit-code mapping, prevents post-failure mutation, and proves same-seed determinism. Reported verification: fmt, diff check, UBS, targeted contract test, clippy test, full cargo check, and full clippy passed via RCH.

CopperDuck 2026-05-03: completed the budget/deadline runtime contract slice in tests/contracts/asupersync_budget.rs. Added deterministic wall-clock deadline math coverage for start/midpoint/deadline/post-deadline budget exhaustion, exact limit/used fields, and diagnostic shape. Reported verification: rustfmt check, targeted asupersync_budget contract tests 4/4, cargo fmt --check, full cargo check, full cargo clippy remote exit 0, UBS, git diff --check, and forbidden-dependency scan passed.

CalmDuck close audit 2026-05-03: all v6h4 acceptance surfaces now have implementation and evidence across the classifier/docs slice, boundary-log runtime evidence, LabRuntime supervised failure coverage, and deterministic budget/deadline coverage. Closing the bead after coordination messages 124 and 126 confirmed the remaining non-overlapping runtime test slices completed.
close_reason: Completed runtime classifier, cancellation, budget, side-effect logging, and deterministic outcome coverage for migrated command paths.

title: Replace mock certificate and claims verification with real manifest/hash checks
description:
## Background

Claims and certificates are mechanical if they parse `claims.yaml`, inspect artifact manifests, verify hashes, check schema versions, and report stale/expired/failed assumptions from explicit fields. They cross the boundary when they use mock certificates or example claim postures.

Audit anchors: `src/core/certificate.rs` uses `create_mock_certificates()`, and claim diagnostics contain example claim postures when a file exists.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Implement or plan real file-backed claim/certificate lookup. If the schema is not complete, return unavailable/degraded. High-level claim review and ambitious artifact design should move to a skill; hash/schema verification stays in Rust.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- `ee claim list/show/verify/diag` parse real claims files and manifests or return degraded file-missing/schema-invalid reports.
- `ee certificate list/show/verify` read real certificate records/manifests; no mock certificates appear in normal output.
- Verification checks payload hash, schema version, expiry, revocation/status, and explicit assumption fields when present.
- Unit tests cover missing file, invalid YAML/JSON, malformed IDs, hash mismatch, expired certificate, stale schema, failed assumptions, and stable ordering.
- E2E scripts log claims file path, artifact manifest paths, checked hashes, schema/golden result, degradation codes, and stdout/stderr isolation.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CopperDuck completed a narrow claims diagnostics slice. src/core/claims.rs now parses real claims.yaml records for ee diag claims and degrades with parse/repair text for invalid existing claim files instead of fabricating claim-001/claim-002/claim-003 sample postures. tests/contracts/claims.rs adds coverage for missing files, valid real records, and invalid existing files with no sample entries. Focused RCH contracts pass; RCH cargo check and clippy pass; cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, and UBS pass. Full cargo test remains blocked by unrelated preflight/tripwire degraded-honesty failure reported in mail id 252. Remaining v76q work: wire claim list/show/verify to real manifests and hash checks.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CopperDuck completed a second narrow claims core slice. src/core/claims.rs now exposes file-backed claim list/show/verify builders that parse real claims.yaml records, validate real claim/policy/evidence/demo IDs, parse manifest.json schema/status/artifacts, and verify artifact BLAKE3 hashes and sizes from disk. tests/contracts/claims.rs now creates real temporary claim workspaces and covers successful manifest verification, hash mismatch failure, and missing manifest degraded failure in addition to the prior diagnostic no-sample coverage. Verification: RCH cargo test --test contracts gate14_core_claim passed 3/3; RCH cargo test --test contracts gate14_diag_claims passed 3/3; focused RCH cargo clippy --test contracts -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check passed; git diff --check on the claims/Beads files passed; UBS on src/core/claims.rs and tests/contracts/claims.rs exited 0 with warnings only. RCH cargo check --all-targets is currently blocked outside this slice by tests/degraded_honesty.rs tripwire_database_arg scope errors in another active reserved surface, so full all-target verification is not clean yet. Remaining v76q work: wire CLI claim list/show/verify to these core builders and complete the certificate half of the bead.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CopperDuck completed the certificate manifest/hash slice. src/core/certificate.rs now consumes explicit ee.certificate.manifest.v1 JSON manifests, filters deterministic certificate lists from real records, shows records by ID, and verifies payload schema, revocation/status, expiry, failed assumptions, and BLAKE3 payload hashes. Payload paths are constrained to manifest-local relative components so unsafe absolute/parent traversal paths produce invalid_manifest instead of filesystem reads. tests/contracts/certificates.rs adds file-backed manifest contracts for list/show/verify and explicit failure modes. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched Rust files passed; git diff --check passed; UBS on touched files exited 0 with no critical findings and the payload-path security warning fixed; RCH cargo fmt --check passed; RCH cargo test core::certificate::tests --lib passed 16/16; RCH cargo test --test contracts certificate_ passed 13/13; RCH cargo check --all-targets passed; RCH cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed. Remaining v76q scope: CLI certificate wiring, claim list/show/verify CLI wiring, and any final e2e logging/golden coverage needed for the full bead acceptance.

comments.author: BoldBridge
comments.text: BoldBridge/cod-pane4 completed a narrow certificate CLI default-manifest slice. src/cli/mod.rs now resolves ee certificate list/show/verify to <workspace>/certificates.json when --manifest is omitted, while preserving certificate_store_unavailable when no manifest exists. Added inline CLI coverage that writes a real manifest + payload and exercises list/show/verify with BLAKE3 hash verification. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/cli/mod.rs passed; UBS src/cli/mod.rs exited 0 with no critical findings. RCH cargo test cli::tests::certificate_commands_read_workspace_default_manifest --lib is blocked by pre-existing dirty src/core/causal.rs compile errors: missing trace_degradation and causal_evidence_unavailable.

comments.author: BoldBridge
comments.text: Follow-up verification note: after later commits, RCH still blocks on src/core/causal.rs, now at committed lines 1776 and 1784 with missing trace_degradation. I attempted to reserve src/core/causal.rs to unblock but Agent Mail reports an active exclusive reservation held by BlueKnoll until 2026-05-04T05:30:55Z, so I am not editing that file.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Mechanical-boundary reconciliation eidetic_engine_cli-o5l2: claims and certificates remain Rust when they parse real manifests, verify hashes, validate schema/status/expiry/assumptions, and degrade on missing or invalid inputs. High-level claim review routes to skill 1ki4. Closure still needs the remaining CLI wiring/e2e pieces noted in prior comments.

title: Replace memory economy seed data with DB-backed metrics and conservative abstention
description:
## Background

Memory economics is mechanical when it scores artifacts from stored usage, freshness, confidence, false alarms, maintenance debt, and tail-risk metadata. It crosses the boundary when it reports fixed seed stats or recommends pruning without real evidence.

Audit anchors: `src/core/economy.rs` contains fixed artifact counts, fixed utility scores, seed prune recommendations, and in-memory budget simulations.

## Acceptance Criteria

See this bead's structured acceptance_criteria field for the detailed completion contract, including unit/static checks, e2e or docs/e2e coverage, detailed logging, and no-feature-loss requirements.
design: Keep formulas, budget allocation, and report-only plans, but require real stored data or explicit fixture mode. Sparse evidence should produce abstain/review, never aggressive demotion. Tail-risk reserves should be explicit metadata, not inferred vibes.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- `ee economy report/score/prune-plan/simulate` read real artifact rows or explicit fixture inputs; normal workspace output contains no seed artifacts.
- Sparse or missing data returns abstain/review recommendations with degradation codes.
- Prune plans remain dry-run/report-only until a separate audited mutation path exists.
- Tail-risk reserves protect safety-critical artifacts even when usage evidence is sparse.
- Unit tests cover empty DB, sparse evidence, false-alarm high, stale artifact, tail-risk protected, deterministic ordering, and no mutation.
- E2E scripts log DB fixture, scored artifact IDs, formula components, mutation posture, schema/golden results, and first-failure diagnosis.
close_reason: Completed DB-backed memory economy metrics and CLI wiring. Commands now read persisted FrankenSQLite memory/feedback data, abstain/review conservatively for empty or sparse evidence, keep prune/simulate/report read-only, protect explicit tail-risk metadata, and no longer emit seed/stub economy output. Verified with Gate 20 core + real-binary CLI contract tests, economy unit tests, cargo fmt/check/clippy, UBS, diff check, and forbidden-dependency audit.

comments.author: ProudBasin
comments.text: Implemented DB-backed memory economy metrics and CLI wiring. Core economy report/score/prune-plan/simulate now read persisted memory rows and feedback from FrankenSQLite, return abstain/review/available status with explicit degradation codes for empty/sparse/unavailable data, and keep prune/simulate/report paths read-only. CLI economy commands now call the core DB-backed implementation with stable ee.response.v1 JSON envelopes and optional --database overrides; missing DB returns unsatisfied_degraded_mode instead of seed/stub output. Added Gate 20 core + real-binary CLI coverage for DB fixture rows, false-alarm feedback, tail-risk reserve protection, empty DB abstention, missing DB degraded error, deterministic IDs, and read-only mutation posture. Verification: rustfmt/cargo fmt --check passed; RCH cargo test --test contracts economy_gate20 -- --nocapture passed 4/4; RCH cargo test core::economy::tests --lib -- --nocapture passed 13/13 before CLI wiring; RCH cargo check --all-targets passed; RCH cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp ubs src/core/economy.rs tests/contracts/economy_gate20.rs src/cli/mod.rs exited 0 with no critical findings; cargo tree forbidden dependency rg audit found no banned crates.

title: EE-421: Implement deterministic `ee situation classify/show/explain --json` over task text and repository fingerprints
description:

Task: Implement deterministic `ee situation classify/show/explain --json` over task text and repository fingerprints

Background and intent:
Recognize task shape deterministically so retrieval, preflight, procedures, fixtures, and replay can route by situation instead of treating every prompt as isolated text.

Dependency context:

Non-negotiable constraints:
Global implementation invariants for this task:
- Keep the CLI local-first, deterministic, and agent-native; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics.
- Preserve the no-Tokio and no-rusqlite constraint unless a later ADR explicitly changes the architecture.
- Use SQLModel Rust plus FrankenSQLite for durable data; do not introduce a second storage stack.

design:

- Use SQLModel Rust and FrankenSQLite only. Migrations must be deterministic, introspectable, and covered by schema drift tests.

Implementation guidance:
- Read the surrounding modules and existing project conventions before editing.
- Prefer the planned crate boundaries from the comprehensive plan: CLI wiring in `ee-cli`, reusable domain/services in `ee-core`, models in `ee-models`, persistence in `ee-db`, output rendering in `ee-output`, and adapters in their own optional crates.
- Keep public contracts stable and versioned. Any data structure that leaves the process through CLI/MCP/hook/export should have a schema and at least one golden fixture.
